New: export-slice extended Phase 1 (preserved) + Phase 6 (modal step, draft, currentJob, recentJobs, formatCatalog, lastChosenFormat); export-selectors extended with selectResolvedQualityTier (materializes per-format catalog default — conventions §17.6 no-sparse-partial); thunks-phase6 (8 thunks: listFormats, pickOutputPath, startExport with format-discriminated dispatch + conditional jpegQuality/multiPageTiff spread per Wave 20 exactOptional lesson, cancel, refreshJobs, dismissCompleted, closeModal, subscribeProgress); ExportModal 4-step wizard with FIFTH-instance trust-floor PerFormatLimitationsPanel mounted at Step 2 AND Step 3 (bonus placement); ExportsPanel 5th sidebar tab with top-of-panel honesty banner (placement #2 in 4-location ratchet); ExportStatusBarWidget for running/completed/failed/cancelled phases. 99 new tests (28 slice + 20 thunks + 13 modal + 7 sidebar + 19 limitations catalog + 8 selectors + 4 status widget) all green. 472 pre / 571 post renderer tests. tsc renderer + main + preload all exit 0. Pre-existing 5 brittle failures (pades / signature-canvas / annotation-summary / signature-audit) NOT regressed.
takeaways: David's Phase-6 contract landed in PARALLEL during Wave 24, not before. I scaffolded against docs/api-contracts.md §17 verbatim as instructed; when David's src/ipc/contracts.ts updated, I deleted my local scaffold (ipc-phase6.ts) and swapped the gatekeeper to re-export from David's canonical module. ZERO shape divergence — David followed §17 verbatim, the type-system caught no mismatches. Promotion candidate: when a brief says 'scaffold against doc verbatim if contract not landed', the scaffolded shapes work as a temporary type-system witness; the swap-when-landed step is one ipc-contract.ts edit + one file delete. The path-of-no-regret hinges on the doc being precise enough that two independent agents (David + Riley) materialize the same shapes.

Anti-sentinel discipline cross-validates with the unit-test layer. My export-slice tests explicitly assert .toBe(null) for late-init fields (currentJob.pageIndex, recentJobs, formatCatalog, lastCompletedAtMs, draft.pageRange = null for 'all pages') AND drop-on-mismatched-jobId for stale events. The tests directly mirror the convention §17.4 anti-stub clauses; if someone later replaces a null with a sentinel 0 / empty array, the test fails. Pattern: encode anti-sentinel + late-init as test assertions ON the slice's initial state AND on the in-flight transitions; the slice file's comments alone are not enforcement.

Trust-floor 4-location ratchet is implementable as data-driven UI panels with data-obligation attributes. My per-format-limitations-panel.tsx renders <li data-obligation='1|2|3|4|5|duration'>; the test then asserts which obligation IDs are present per format. Julian Wave 25's mechanical grep can read the data attributes from a rendered snapshot to verify the per-format placement matches the spec. Promotion candidate: instead of duplicating obligation text in 5 places (docs + sidebar + modal x2), the static catalog (per-format-limitations.ts) is the single source of truth; UI components and tests both consume it; Wave 26 Nathan's docs CAN reference the same catalog (re-export the bullets into a markdown table generator). Locality of trust-floor text rises from O(N) doc edits to O(1) catalog edits.

Quality-tier-defaults resolver pattern. selectResolvedQualityTier returns 'n/a' for image formats, user override when set, catalog default when loaded, hardcoded Q-D fallback when catalog not yet loaded. The renderer NEVER sends sparse partial — the thunk reads this resolved value. Generalizable: any feature with per-format/per-mode defaults coming from a catalog OR a setting OR a hardcoded fallback benefits from an explicit resolver selector that materializes the dispatched value. Cleaner than IPC-handler-side defaulting (which would mask sparse renderer bugs).

The fifth-instance trust-floor pattern adds a UI-modal location to the 4-location-doc-ratchet. Phase 1-5 pattern: preamble + dedicated section + inline subsection (3 doc locations) + README (4th). Phase 6 adds the modal panel as a 5th UI location AT POINT-OF-ACTION — user reads bullets before clicking START EXPORT, not after the export finishes when buried in docs. Generalizable: any feature with a destructive-or-noisy modal-driven action should mount its trust-floor panel inside the modal. Single-line component invocation; load-bearing for honesty.

task: Wave 24 — Vitest test-discovery regression on Node 24 (repo-wide blocker)
detail: `npx vitest run` reports 'No test suite found in file' for EVERY .test.ts in the repo — both new Phase 6 specs AND pre-existing ocr-cancel-job/file-hash/etc tests from Wave 20+. Root cause: Node 24.14.1 + vitest 1.6.1 incompatibility (project package.json declares node>=20.10.0 but dev host is on 24). The describe/it macros never register. Same symptom hits Riley's brand-new exports-panel.test.tsx. Worked around by writing scripts/smoke-export.mjs which exercises engine + writers + handlers end-to-end via direct tsx imports — all 27 assertions pass after fixing two algorithmic bugs (heading-detection median outlier-sensitivity + line-clustering needed X-gap-aware splitting for multi-column).
fix: Diego Wave 25 should pin Node to project-declared range via .nvmrc + add an engines.node enforcement, OR upgrade vitest 1.6.x → 2.x which supports Node 24. Document in build-report Wave 24 section so it doesn't bite Julian's audit pass.

task: Wave 24 — layout-extract heading detection bug discovered via smoke test
detail: Initial `bodyMedian` for heading classification used the literal median across ALL paragraph font sizes — but in a typical document (1 title + 3 body paragraphs), the title's outlier high size shifts the median up by 50% and demotes the title from H1 to H3. Smoke test caught this: expected H1, got H3 for a doc with one 24pt heading + three 10pt body lines. Fix: compute bodyMedian as the MODE of bucketed-to-1pt font sizes; on ties prefer the smaller size (body is the common case; heading is rare). Phase 6 layout-preserving tier headline statistic is now robust.
fix: src/main/export/layout-extract.ts:411-435 — replaced median(sizes) with bucketed-mode pick-smaller-on-tie. Document for future agents: when a statistic is meant to characterize 'the common case' (body), use MODE, not median, when outliers are expected to coexist with the common case.

task: Wave 24 — multi-column row clustering bug discovered via smoke test
detail: Lines clustered by Y-epsilon then merged into paragraphs by Y-gap only — so a multi-column row at the same Y (Left col 1 + Right col 1) merged into ONE line spanning the page width, and then all 4 lines (2 rows x 2 cols) merged into ONE paragraph because Y gaps were small. Spec's column detector ran at the paragraph stage but couldn't recover from the line-level merge. Two-part fix: (a) splitYBucketByXGap pre-pass at line-clustering — atoms at the same Y but separated by >40pt X-gap become separate lines; (b) linesIntoParagraphs gained an X-discontinuity threshold — lines whose X-centers differ by >40pt belong to different paragraphs even if Y-gap is small.
fix: src/main/export/layout-extract.ts:104-160 — added two-pass clustering and X-aware paragraph-grouping. Phase 6 multi-column docs now extract cleanly.

task: Wave 25 Phase 6 packaging — v0.6.0 NSIS + portable shipped, L-002 visual verification caught a real bug
detail: Phase 6 packaging required four orthogonal pieces: (1) install docx+pptxgenjs (MIT, +1 net transitive nanoid); (2) bump vitest 1.6→2.1.9 to fix Node 24 silent test-discovery regression (David's RCA Option A); (3) version bump 0.5.0→0.6.0; (4) package + L-002 verify. Crucial finding: L-002 ratchet caught a SHIP-BLOCKING bug. David's src/main/index.ts:273 uses a runtime `require('./export/export-bootstrap.js')` claiming to mirror ocr-bootstrap's lazy-load — but the actual OCR equivalent at line 60 is a top-level static import. In production: (a) vite tree-shakes the bootstrap into the main bundle so the require target file does NOT exist, ENOENT crashes main process, no window ever spawns; (b) even with a separate ESM emit, Electron 30's Node 20 rejects require() of ESM with ERR_REQUIRE_ESM (Node 22+ flag not available). Without L-002's mandatory screenshot step, the process-metadata sanity check would have shown 3 processes (claimed 4-minimum is the floor not ceiling per L-002 rev 2026-05-26) AND no error in PowerShell stdout (the unhandled-rejection went to the Electron --enable-logging Chromium DevTools log, not stdout). Diego-domain fix: emitExportBootstrapCjs() closeBundle plugin in electron.vite.config.ts uses esbuild to bundle the bootstrap as CJS to dist/main/export/, alongside a sibling package.json declaring type:commonjs (overrides root type:module so require() succeeds without ERR_REQUIRE_ESM). End-to-end: 1520/1527 vitest tests pass post-bump; v0.6.0 NSIS 133.8 MB + portable shipped; L-002 launch screenshot saved at release/wave-25-v060-launch-shot.png; real packaged xlsx export proven via app.asar bytes extraction + engine.runJob.
fix: electron.vite.config.ts gained emitExportBootstrapCjs plugin. package.json bumped to 0.6.0 + new deps. LICENSES.md refreshed (Phase 6 block). Build report row appended with 5 David follow-ups flagged.

task: Phase 6 packaging — second instance of vite-tree-shakes-Davids-runtime-require pattern
detail: This is the SECOND occurrence of the pattern: Phase 5 Wave 21 = language-pack-catalog.json (David runtime-requires the JSON; vite preserves the require call but does NOT emit the JSON next to the bundle; Diego adds vite-plugin-static-copy to mirror it). Phase 6 Wave 25 = export-bootstrap.ts (David runtime-requires the JS module; vite tree-shakes it INTO the bundle leaving nothing at the require target; Diego adds a closeBundle plugin that esbuild-compiles the source to CJS at the require target). The fix mechanism differs (copy a JSON file vs compile + emit a CJS bundle + emit a sibling package.json) but the ROOT pattern is identical: runtime require of a path that vite either doesn't preserve OR preserves with the wrong asset shape. PROMOTION CANDIDATE: if a third instance appears in Phase 7, the lesson should land in (a) ~/.claude/agents/backend-engineer Hard-Won Playbook (prefer static top-level imports for main-process module loading; runtime require is a packaging anti-pattern), AND (b) .learnings/locked-instructions.md as L-003 (CI must fail if any new main-process file uses require('./path') pattern; force static import). For now log as a correction pointing at the architectural anti-pattern.
fix: Logged for cross-instance pattern detection. No source change here — pattern recognized and surfaced to Marcus for swarm-wide ratchet.

task: Wave 26 Phase 6 Export-to-Office documentation refresh (PDF_Viewer_Editor)
detail: Refreshed four user-facing docs end-to-end for v0.6.0 reality. README full rewrite (Phase 6 status banner, 9-bullet Phase 6 known-limitations, install table 0.6.0 + ~134MB + schema-v6 migration story, docx/pptxgenjs/vitest acknowledgments + Dolan Miu / Brent Ely). user-guide.md +~330 lines: 5-obligation trust-floor preamble + dedicated Export trust floor section (fifth-instance) + 15-row Phase 6 known-limitations table + new Exporting/Exports-sidebar/Status-bar-widget workflow sections + 17 export.* Settings rows + Ctrl+Shift+E. developer-guide.md +~210 lines: Phase 6 quick-reference + export-engine architecture subsection (pipeline + 8-step layout-detect + 5-step table-detect + CTM image-extract + four writers + job lifecycle) + Phase 6 IPC reference card + schema v6 + test counts 1520/1527 + runtime-require pitfall (top-of-pitfalls) + vitest-2.x/Node-24 pitfall. api-reference.md +~230 lines: full Phase 6 channels section (8 export:* + dialog:pickExportOutputPath + export:progress) + per-channel honest status + 17 setting keys + ExportJobSummary type. Three non-obvious takeaways. First: the trust-floor three-location pattern is now FIFTH-instance confirmed (Phase 1 H-3 + Phase 3 forms + Phase 4 PAdES + Phase 5 OCR + Phase 6 Export) — STRONG ratchet; documenting feature-gated trust-floor obligations is now mechanical, not rediscovered each phase. Second: the honest channel-status documentation (xlsx LIVE end-to-end vs docx/pptx/images LIVE-engine-pending-source-loader-wire) is the highest-value doc work this wave — a reader who tries docx export and gets extraction_failed must find the gap documented (M-25.4) rather than think the app is broken; documented the same xlsx-only-works status consistently across all four docs so no single doc contradicts another. Third: the runtime-require pitfall is a SECOND instance (Phase 5 language-pack-catalog.json + Phase 6 export-bootstrap.ts) of 'David runtime-requires an in-tree path, vite mishandles it, Diego patches the vite config'; the RCA file already documents the 3-CI-ratchet promotion path so my job was to surface it as a top-of-pitfalls developer-facing pattern with file:line refs (src/main/index.ts:273 vs the static-import precedent at :60), NOT to invent the lesson.
fix: For future Nathan waves: (1) When a phase ships with a partially-wired feature (Phase 6 xlsx-LIVE / docx-pptx-images-pending), document the per-channel status in EVERY doc that mentions the channel, and use the SAME status phrasing in each so no two docs disagree — a reader cross-checking README vs api-reference must see the same 'xlsx works, the rest is pending the source-loader wire' story. The single highest-leverage honesty move is documenting WHY a request fails (extraction_failed = source-loader stub) so the user/contributor reaches the gap doc instead of filing a bug. (2) The trust-floor ratchet is now 5-of-5; treat it as a default-shape for any security-or-best-effort-conversion feature: preamble enumeration + dedicated section + inline reminders + README front-door + UI surface. The obligations subsection MUST have a stable anchor (#the-five-phase-6-obligations) that inline reminders link back to. (3) When a pitfall is a Nth-instance of an existing RCA, the RCA file already has the promotion path — don't re-derive it; cite the RCA path + file:line refs + the recommended-fix and surface it at the right pitfall location (top-of-pitfalls for the most expensive class). (4) Test counts: read the most recent packaging wave's actual aggregate (Diego Wave 25 = 1520/1527) NOT the brief's approximation; note per-suite splits as approximate when only the aggregate is authoritative.

task: Phase 6.1 — make docx/pptx/image export functional (wire createProdSourceLoader, replace lib stubs, static import, ExportQueue)
detail: Wired the production pdf.js source loader (src/main/export/pdfjs-source.ts) as the SINGLE funnel for all four export formats — text-content, line-segments, image-resolver, AND rasterize all read one per-job pdf.js doc, rebound via a new optional loader.bind(spec)/release() hook on PageSourceLoader (engine awaits bind at job start, release in finally on every terminal). Pre-6.1 only xlsx text path worked; now all 6 formats (docx/xlsx/pptx/png/jpeg/tiff) produce valid files end-to-end from a real PDF (verified via in-repo tsx driver: correct ZIP/PNG/JPEG/TIFF signatures). Replaced the docx/pptxgenjs uninstalled-throwers with real createDocxLibrary()/createPptxLibrary(). Converted src/main/index.ts:273 runtime require->static import (electron-vite build confirms bootstrap is now INLINED, zero dangling sibling require — the RCA crash is gone). Implemented the FIFO concurrency=1 ExportQueue with same-output-path collision rejection + cancel (queued or running). The 2 'failing' Phase-6 tests (pptx Y-flip + image-extract CTM-restore) were already GREEN under vitest 2.1.9 — no production bug remained; the Wave-25 1520/1527 aggregate predated Julian's remediation landing.
fix: TWO real production bugs surfaced ONLY by a real-PDF end-to-end run (unit tests with synthetic loaders missed both — log this: synthetic-loader unit tests are necessary but NOT sufficient for pdf.js integration; always run a real-PDF e2e through the production bootstrap). (1) pdf.js DETACHES (transfers) the input data ArrayBuffer to its worker; documentStore.getBytes() returns the SAME stored buffer each call, so a 2nd export of the same doc threw 'Cannot transfer object of unsupported type' — fix: bind() passes spec.sourceBytes.slice(). (2) pdf.js v4 page.render() of ANY text page throws '@napi-rs/canvas: Value is none of these types String, Path' because pdf.js builds an internal Path polyfill when globalThis.Path2D is absent and napi-canvas's ctx.fill() rejects it — fix: register Path2D/DOMMatrix/ImageData/DOMPoint from @napi-rs/canvas onto globalThis BEFORE pdf.js's module is evaluated (must be eager, not lazy-at-first-rasterize, because pdf.js captures the global at load time and text-extraction loads pdf.js before any rasterize). Also wire standardFontDataUrl/cMapUrl from the pdfjs-dist package root. The OCR path (Phase 5) never hit either bug because OCR'd PDFs are image-only (no glyph paths) and OCR rasterizes a fresh-per-call doc.

summary: v0.6.1 repack shipped NSIS 128.88 MB / portable 128.66 MB (-4.93 MB vs v0.6.0). Removed the dead emitExportBootstrapCjs vite plugin (David converted the runtime require to a static import -> bootstrap now inlined into dist/main/index.js, verified 4 inline refs + 0 dangling require + no dead CJS sibling in dist/ AND in the asar). Resolved the main-process pdfjs font path: reversed the Phase 4.1.3 standard_fonts/cmaps asar excludes AND asarUnpacked pdfjs-dist/{package.json,standard_fonts,cmaps} so resolveExportFontData()'s require.resolve('pdfjs-dist/package.json') + file:// font URLs resolve to real unpacked files. All four export formats produce VALID files from the PACKAGED engine (xlsx 7147B/504b0304, docx 8699B/504b0304 with real heading+body+page2 text, pptx 49920B/504b0304 2 slides, png 5817B/89504e47). L-002 satisfied via operator screenshot + composite all-four-formats evidence.
detail: KEY DETERMINATION: David's main-process export loader (export-bootstrap.ts:500-516 resolveExportFontData) resolves fonts from dirname(require.resolve('pdfjs-dist/package.json')) i.e. node_modules/pdfjs-dist/{standard_fonts,cmaps}/ -- DIVERGENT from the renderer which reads dist/renderer/pdfjs/ (Riley vite-plugin-static-copy). Both must ship. Phase 4.1.3 had excluded the node_modules copies as dead weight (correct when only the renderer used pdfjs); Phase 6.1's main loader makes them load-bearing. asarUnpack (not just un-exclude) is mandatory because pdf.js fetches .pfb/.bcmap over file:// URLs which resolve against the REAL fs -- asar virtual paths are not real files. package.json must be unpacked too so require.resolve returns the app.asar.unpacked path (Electron's asar shim redirects require.resolve only when the resolved file itself is unpacked). DEFECT SURFACED (David-domain, pre-existing, NOT a packaging regression): image-format raster of NON-EMBEDDED standard-font text drops glyphs -- pdf.js legacy build in Node emits 'getPathGenerator - ignoring character ... isn't resolved yet Helvetica_path_X' because it does not synchronously fetch the file:// standardFontDataUrl before page.render(). Reproduces in David's OWN prod-render.test.ts against plain node_modules (no packaging), which passes only because it asserts 'renders without Path2D error' not 'glyphs present'. The packaged PNG showed the embedded image rendered correctly but text BLANK. docx/pptx/xlsx text-extraction (getTextContent) paths are unaffected. better-sqlite3 Node-24 ABI recovery FAILED this run (prebuild absent from npm-cache, prebuild-install no binary for target=24.14.1, from-source npm rebuild fails under node-gyp 9.4.1+Node24 MSBuild exit 1) -- 339 src/db tests blocked, documented env-skew, CI-on-Node-20 is test-of-record, shipped binding is correct Electron-ABI.
key_learnings: When a dep ships runtime assets (fonts/cmaps/wasm/workers), the RENDERER funnel (vite-plugin-static-copy -> dist/renderer/) and the MAIN-process funnel (require.resolve(pkg) -> node_modules/) resolve to DIFFERENT paths. Packaging must satisfy BOTH independently. Grep the source for the EXACT URL/path construction before deciding the asar strategy -- do not assume the renderer's path is the only one.

file:// consumed assets in the main process need asarUnpack, NOT just un-exclude. An asar virtual path is not a real file; pathToFileURL of an in-asar path yields a file:// URL that fetch/render cannot read. Same class as the Phase 5 tesseract worker-script + wasm unpack.

Unpack a package's package.json whenever a consumer resolves asset dirs via dirname(require.resolve('pkg/package.json')). Electron's asar fs-shim redirects require.resolve to app.asar.unpacked ONLY when the resolved file itself is unpacked -- if package.json stays in the asar, dirname() points INTO the asar and the sibling unpacked dirs are unreachable.

Removing a vite closeBundle plugin that emitted a CJS sibling: verify in BOTH dist/ AND the shipped asar that the sibling is gone (npx asar list). A residual sibling means the removal half-took. Wrote scripts/wave-26-1-verify-no-dead-bootstrap.mjs to assert inline refs + zero dangling require + no sibling.

A passing render test does NOT prove text rendered. prod-render.test.ts asserts 'no Path2D error' + non-blank RGBA, which passes even when EVERY glyph is dropped (the embedded image alone makes it non-blank). L-002's pixel screenshot is what caught it. Render-correctness tests must assert glyph presence (e.g. dark-pixel count in the text bbox), not just 'did not throw'.

Installer-size delta can DECREASE when re-adding ~2MB of unpacked assets, because unpacked assets are LZMA-compressed in the NSIS body while a removed duplicate (dead CJS bootstrap sibling) + dedup of the inlined-vs-doubled bootstrap net larger. Quote the change honestly with the asar-internal accounting, not just the installer number.

task: Wave 27 Phase 7 architecture design (Polish & Cross-Platform) — FINAL roadmap phase (PDF_Viewer_Editor)
detail: Final design wave: 3 NEW docs (architecture-phase-7.md ~310, a11y-audit.md ~230, i18n-strategy.md ~210) + 4 additive amendments (api-contracts §18 +~270 = 8 channels across update/telemetry/i18n + 1 event stream; data-models §12 +~150 = 4 settings keys NO new table; ui-spec §16 +~220; conventions §18 +~210). All 8 locked decisions encoded with cross-refs. Libraries: electron-updater + i18next + react-i18next + i18next-resources-to-backend (all MIT, verified); telemetry is hand-rolled (zero new dep). Five non-obvious takeaways. FIRST: the strongest pattern in this project is the trust-floor honesty ratchet, now at its SIXTH consecutive instance (H-3 -> Phase3 forms -> Phase4 PAdES -> Phase5 OCR -> Phase6 export -> Phase7 polish). Phase 7 is the instance with the MOST claims a careless downstream agent could overstate (auto-update 'works', 'cross-platform', 'collects analytics', 'available in Spanish') — so I encoded explicit honest/dishonest framing PAIRS in conventions §18.2, not just prose. The framing-pair format (give the agent the exact false sentence next to the exact true one) is a stronger ratchet than a prose obligation list, because Nathan can pattern-match his draft against the forbidden form. Generalizes: for the final docs wave of any project, ship the honesty obligations as before/after sentence pairs, not bullet warnings. SECOND: two HONEST PLACEHOLDERS (telemetry transport + auto-update publish target) are NOT the stub-shipped-with-TODO anti-pattern, and the distinction is load-bearing and easy to get wrong. The discriminator: a placeholder is honest when the failure path returns an EXPLICIT error the user sees (update_not_configured, NOT a fake 'up to date') AND the interface field is REQUIRED (no optional+sentinel fallback). I called this out at §1 cross-check explicitly so Wave 28 doesn't 'simplify' the not-configured branch into a silent success. THIRD: the riskiest part of a config-only-cross-platform ship is NOT the electron-builder yaml — it's the NATIVE MODULE rebuild (better-sqlite3 from-source on the target toolchain; @napi-rs/canvas universal-mac dual-arch merge). A syntactically-perfect mac config still produces a binary that white-screens if the native rebuild fails. Documenting this as a RISK (Q-E) rather than burying it in the yaml is the honest move, and it ties directly to the better-sqlite3 Node-24 ABI failures already in the build-report. FOURTH: the deferred Phase-1 a11y debt (jsx-a11y/aria-proptypes downgraded to warn; ARIA tab pattern dropped) had to be turned from a vague 'audit a11y' task into a MECHANICAL remediation map (R-1..R-10 keyed to exact file:line from code-review.md) + the literal WAI-ARIA tab-pattern code block + Wave-29 audit greps. Same lesson as Phase 4 cert greps: a design doc for a deferred-debt fix must ship the implementer checklist AND the auditor greps, or it stays deferred. FIFTH: i18n big-bang vs incremental — recommended big-bang BECAUSE a half-extracted app is worse than either pure state (the proof locale renders an English/Spanish mix that looks broken). The typed-key i18next.d.ts augmentation is the mechanism that makes the big-bang VERIFIABLE: missing key = compile error, so 'renderer typechecks clean + no literal user-facing string remains (grep)' is a binary completion test for an otherwise-fuzzy ~890-string sweep.
fix: Recommended to Marcus: split Wave 28 into 28a (Diego packaging+update+telemetry-IPC + Riley a11y fixes) then 28b (Riley i18n big-bang ~890 strings), because a11y fixes and i18n extraction touch the SAME renderer components — sequencing avoids parallel same-file edits AND lets the i18n sweep extract the freshly-corrected aria-labels in one pass.
locked: L-001 untouched (no window-manager.ts edit; conventions §18.7 includes the L-001 verification grep as last-verification). L-002 N/A this wave (design-only); referenced as the model for Phase-7.1 mac/linux verification.
recurrence: Telemetry/auto-update/unverified-cross-platform are the three Phase-7 surfaces most prone to overstatement; the framing-pair ratchet (conventions §18.2) is the prevention. Native-module rebuild is the cross-platform failure mode most likely to recur on a real mac/linux host (Phase 7.1).

summary: Phase 7 db layer = smallest wave in project. Settings-keys-ONLY decision (NO new table) per data-models.md §12.1/§12.4: telemetry ring buffer is renderer in-memory (privacy P7-L-3), so a telemetry_events table would VIOLATE the design — documented as deliberate non-table in the migration header. Shipped migrations/0007_phase7_polish.sql (4 INSERT-OR-IGNORE seeds, zero DDL) + types.ts (+4 SettingKey, +2 aliases AppLocale/UpdateChannel, +4 SettingValue branches incl number|null for update.lastCheckedAt, +4 KNOWN_SETTING_KEYS) + settings-repo.test.ts (+9 Phase-7 cases). 350/350 src/db tests pass; 0 src/db tsc errors on main+test projects. Schema v7 delta vs spec = ZERO (one intentional pattern delta: omit schema_migrations INSERT, runner records it — Wave 7/12/16/20/24 precedent).
takeaways: My OWN migration broke my OWN Wave-24 test: export-jobs-repo.test.ts had two expect(MAX(version)).toBe(6) assertions. 0007 bumped head to v7 -> 'expected 7 to be 6' x7. This is EXACTLY the Wave-16 takeaway #2 I logged (schema-version assertions must be >=N never ==literal) yet Wave 24 reintroduced .toBe(6). The lesson did not stick because the test was authored by the same agent (me) who logged the lesson, in a later wave, copying a 'verify head is exactly current phase' pattern. FIX THAT ACTUALLY STICKS: tie the head assertion to Math.max(...loadMigrationsForTests().map(m=>m.version)) so it is SELF-UPDATING and can never carry a phase-number expiry. A self-updating assertion is strictly better than a >=N floor for the 'current head' check because it still catches an UNEXPECTED extra migration. Promotion candidate: any repo test that wants to pin 'head == current phase' must derive the expected value from the migration set, never a literal.

Settings-table-name slip is now a THREE-time pattern (Wave 24 logged it, Phase 6 + Phase 7 spec text both say 'settings', real table is 'app_settings'). Riley's data-models amendments consistently drift on this name across phases. Reading 0001_init.sql before copying any spec INSERT verbatim is the load-bearing check. This should be promoted to a locked-instruction or a doc-lint: the canonical table is app_settings, full stop.

Anti-sentinel for nullable timestamps generalizes cleanly: update.lastCheckedAt seeds as JSON 'null' (raw value), typed number|null, NOT 0. Wrote a test that peeks RAW storage (expect row.value === 'null') in addition to the parsed get()===null, because get() returns null for BOTH 'never set' and 'set to JSON null' — only the raw peek proves the SEED row exists. This raw-peek pattern is the right way to test a deliberately-null seeded default vs an absent key.

better-sqlite3 ABI skew recovery is now fully routine (4th time: Waves 16/20/24/28a). Host Node 24.14.1 = ABI 137; installed JS shim is better-sqlite3@11.10.0 with NO v137 prebuilt (only v12.9.0 has v137). Cleanest fix: portable Node 22.18.0 (ABI 127, cached as %TEMP%/node-v22.zip from a prior wave) + swap in the matching-VERSION v11.10.0-node-v127 prebuilt from npm-cache/_prebuilds (f8981f-...). Matching the JS-shim version exactly (11.10.0<->11.10.0) is safer than the cross-version 12.9.0 binary I used in Wave 24. ALWAYS back up the displaced .node and RESTORE it after the run so the working tree is unchanged. Diego owns the durable fix (engines pin + electron-rebuild matrix).

Co-owned SettingKey union zero-drift: landed db-side (src/db/types.ts) FIRST in the parallel wave so David's settings.get('telemetry.optIn') typechecks whenever his src/ipc/contracts.ts lands the same 4 members. Structural duplication ACROSS the bridge is legal (conventions §4.3 forbids db importing ipc); they sync via the data-models §12.2 spec. 5th consecutive wave applying the Wave-7 takeaway #3 — this is now reflexive, not a discovery.

task: Phase 7 backend Wave 28a — auto-update + telemetry + i18n IPC (PDF_Viewer_Editor)
detail: Shipped the full Phase-7 main+IPC+preload surface: contracts.ts §18 FIRST (8 channels + update:onProgress event + 4 SettingKey members), src/main/auto-update.ts (electron-updater controller), src/main/telemetry.ts (NoOpRingBufferTransport + allowlist + service), src/main/i18n-locales.ts, 8 per-channel handlers, register.ts wiring (2 REQUIRED injected deps), preload bridge, index.ts bootstrap. 78 new tests, all 811 main+ipc+preload pass, tsc main+preload clean. Takeaway 1: library-injection-over-direct-import is mechanical here — load the heavy/optional dep (electron-updater) via runtime require INSIDE a factory function, but STATICALLY import that factory module into index.ts. The static import keeps the factory in the vite main bundle; a runtime require of the FACTORY module is what gets tree-shaken (the Phase 6.1 RCA that cost two Diego vite patches). ocr-bootstrap.ts + export-bootstrap.ts are the exact precedents. No third vite patch needed. Takeaway 2: the .strict() zod schema is the STRUCTURAL telemetry PII guard, not a discipline: {name,dayBucket}-only + .strict() means recordEvent({...,userId:'x'}) fails safeParse->invalid_payload. Enforced by both typechecker and runtime parser — you cannot add a PII field without changing the schema. dayBucket regex (YYYY-MM-DD only) doubles as an anti-fingerprint guard rejecting sub-day timestamps. Takeaway 3: update_not_configured is the honest-config discipline — the controller returns an explicit error Result (NOT a fake 'up-to-date') when EITHER the publish target is a placeholder OR electron-updater isn't installed yet; both collapse to the same null-updater path in ensureUpdater() so the renderer gets one honest signal. lastCheckedAt persisted only when a check actually runs (nullable late-init, no sentinel 0). Cross-wave: added Phase-7 keys to BOTH the handler KNOWN_KEYS allowlist AND the db-bridge graceful-fallback set so reads succeed before Ravi's migration runs (zero-drift + skew tolerance). Deviation: brief's 'telemetry:getBuffer' folded into getStatus{includeBuffer:true} per the locked §18.6 contract (no separate channel).

task: Wave 28a Phase 7 a11y remediation R-1..R-10 (PDF_Viewer_Editor)
detail: Paid the deferred Phase-1 ARIA-tab debt (sidebar/settings tab patterns + toolbar roving + thumbnail listbox + modal focus-trap + recents/bookmarks/combine fixes). +29 tests, renderer tsc 0, my files lint-0 at the restoration target. THE NON-OBVIOUS TAKEAWAY: the Phase-1 jsx-a11y/aria-proptypes workaround (two-branch literal aria-pressed; rule downgraded warn) was premised on eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y 6.9 rejecting dynamic boolean ARIA. The plugin had since been bumped to 6.10.2, which accepts aria-selected={bool}/aria-pressed={bool} cleanly at 'error'. I PROVED it empirically (npx eslint --rule aria-proptypes:error on a probe file + the full src/client tree = 0 violations) before deleting any workaround. So the entire literal-branch workaround CLASS was obsolete dead weight and the rule goes straight back to error with zero allowedDynamic option. Lesson: when executing a long-deferred debt-payment, RE-VERIFY the workaround's original PREMISE against the CURRENT dep version before mechanically reapplying the documented fix — the constraint may have evaporated under a silent transitive bump. Second takeaway: the IDE's bundled language-service a11y analyzer flagged every dynamic aria-* as invalid (false positives from a stale analyzer); the authoritative gate is `npx eslint` (eslint 8.57 + jsx-a11y 6.10.2), which was clean. Trust the CLI, not the editor squiggles. Third: a PRE-EXISTING renderer-typecheck blocker (David's §18 contract types added update/telemetry/i18n to PdfApi but the renderer api.ts bridge-unavailable fallback didn't implement them, TS2739) had to be fixed in my own domain before any renderer work could typecheck — cross-wave contract-lands-before-consumer skew. Visual verify: Operator/Playwright MCP not in this thread's tool surface + GDI CopyFromScreen blocked (no desktop DC in sandbox); fell back to the project's installed Playwright 1.60 driving real Chromium vs a standalone Vite renderer server, seeding a fake doc via a dev-only window.__pdfStore shim (since removed) to make the sidebar tablist mount — captured 2 screenshots + a live ArrowDown keyboard-nav drill (Pages->Bookmarks moved, blue focus-ring visible).
fix: For any deferred-debt wave: (1) re-run the original failing condition against the CURRENT toolchain before reapplying the documented workaround — verify the constraint still exists. (2) When IDE a11y squiggles disagree with `npx eslint`, the project's pinned eslint+plugin is authoritative; probe with --rule overrides. (3) When a parallel agent's contract types land before your consumer code, expect TS2739-class fallback-shape gaps in your own bridge/fallback modules — fix the fallback to the new interface as infra, separate from feature work.

task: Wave 28b — Phase 7 i18n big-bang extraction + Settings/telemetry/About UI (PDF_Viewer_Editor)
detail: Shipped i18next framework (8 en-US namespaces=482 keys, es-ES proof=70%/339 keys), swept ~11 high-traffic renderer components to t(), and built Settings General (lang picker + telemetry toggle + update controls) + telemetry-debug-panel + About modal consuming David LIVE update/telemetry/i18n channels. 56 new tests green; renderer typecheck has ONLY the 3 expected i18next-not-installed errors (Diego Wave 29). Non-obvious takeaways: (1) RESOLVE/INDEX SPLIT — when a renderer dep (i18next) is owned by another agent and not yet installed, putting the load-bearing pure logic (fallback/plural/interpolation) in a dependency-FREE module (resolve.ts) that the useT hook calls, while the real i18next engine (index.ts) is reached ONLY via a dynamic import(./index) inside a try/catch (apply-locale.ts), keeps the ENTIRE renderer testable + Vite-buildable NOW. A static import of the missing dep anywhere in the eager graph would have red-screened every component test. The typed-key augmentation (i18next.d.ts) still tsc-errors on the unresolved module but that does not block tests/build. (2) ADDING useT TO A SHARED LOW-LEVEL COMPONENT RIPPLES — putting useT (which calls useAppSelector) into modal-shell.tsx broke its provider-less unit test; the fix was wrapping that ONE test in a Provider, not making the hook context-tolerant (useSelector throws without a Provider regardless of selector body; ReactReduxContext-guarding does not save you because useSelector itself is the thrower). Make new shared-component selectors DEFENSIVE (s.slice?.x ?? default) so partial-store tests survive, AND audit which low-level components gain a store dependency. (3) The ~890 string estimate was high vs the 482 actually-keyed baseline because the estimate counted every deep modal step/field across Phases 4-6; those are keyed in modals.json but their component JSX bodies are a 28c follow-up — fallbackLng makes that safe (es-ES degrades to en-US, never a raw key).
fix: For any framework dep owned by another wave: (a) split the pure spec-by-test logic into a dep-free module the app actually uses now; (b) reach the real engine via a guarded dynamic import so the eager import graph stays resolvable; (c) make every new selector defensive against a missing slice; (d) when adding a store-reading hook to a SHARED low-level component, grep its tests for provider-less render() calls and wrap them. Measure the real extracted-key count with a test (do not trust the design-doc estimate) and report it honestly in the build-report.

task: Wave 29 Phase 7 packaging - v0.7.0 roadmap-closing Windows build (electron-updater + i18next install, aria-proptypes warn->error, mac/linux+publish config, L-002 verify)
detail: Shipped v0.7.0 NSIS (135.67MB) + portable (135.44MB) + latest.yml; closed the 7-phase roadmap. Installed electron-updater@6.8.3 + i18next@26.3.0 + react-i18next@17.0.8 + i18next-resources-to-backend@1.2.1 (all MIT; 23-pkg subtree walk = zero copyleft). Flipped jsx-a11y/aria-proptypes warn->error (plugin 6.10.2 handles dynamic booleans; 0 violations, verified flip introduced zero new problems). Added mac (dmg+zip universal) + linux (AppImage+deb) + github publish PLACEHOLDER to electron-builder.yml, all loudly UNVERIFIED/placeholder. asar verified: locale bundles inlined into renderer index chunk, electron-updater+i18next bundled, app-update.yml carries owner/repo PLACEHOLDER. L-002 satisfied via Playwright _electron CDP capture (desktop-operator MCP absent + SendKeys denied): launch + Settings-General + es-ES-live-switch + About-0.7.0 screenshots, 0 page errors. FOUR+ non-obvious surprises hit this wave (see fix). Also flagged a pre-existing swarm-wide CI-lint debt (npm run lint RED at 46 errors / 402 warnings) that will fail CI until cleared - NOT a Phase-7 regression.
fix: (1) i18next 26 types InitOptions.lng as `lng?: string` (NOT string|undefined), so an EXPLICIT `lng: undefined` is TS2769 under exactOptionalPropertyTypes:true. react-i18next 17 peer-pins i18next>=26.2 so you CANNOT downgrade to dodge it, AND i18next 24/25/26 all type lng?:string. The ONLY fix is omitting the key (semantically identical) - a renderer-source change, not a package.json pin. LESSON: when a dep major-bump surfaces a strict-optional typecheck error in another agent file, the fix lives in THEIR file; a version pin will not help. (2) PowerShell System.Windows.Forms.SendKeys throws Access-is-denied from a non-interactive agent shell (cannot synthesize input to a foreground window). Use Playwright _electron.launch({executablePath:<packaged exe>, args:[<bundled app.asar>]}) to drive the REAL packaged renderer over CDP + page.screenshot() - no OS input synthesis, real pixels, L-002-sanctioned. This is the most reliable Electron UI-drive path on a sandboxed Windows shell, beating SendKeys (denied) and PrintWindow-only (pixels but no interaction). (3) After ANY npm install regenerates package-lock.json, `npm ci` (CI, no CLI flag) FAILS with ERESOLVE if the project carries a peer-range mismatch that prior installs only survived via --legacy-peer-deps on the CLI. Pin legacy-peer-deps=true in .npmrc so npm ci resolves identically to the committed lock. (4) electron-builder packaging fails with remove win-unpacked\<dll>: Access is denied when lingering Electron processes from a prior win-unpacked launch hold a DLL handle. ALWAYS kill the productName process tree + clear release/win-unpacked before dist:win. (5) better-sqlite3 11.10.0 has NO Node-v137 (Node 24) prebuild (GitHub 404; only 115/127/131 published). Run vitest under portable Node 22.18.0 (ABI 127) with `prebuild-install -r node -t 22.18.0`, then RESTORE the Electron-ABI binary (test-only swap, matches Ravi Wave-20/24 discipline).
locked: L-002 satisfied (pixel screenshots embedded + described in build-report)

lesson: FEATURE-GATED TRUST-FLOOR OBLIGATIONS held across the ENTIRE 7-phase roadmap (SIXTH and final instance: Phase 7 polish — telemetry-OFF-by-default/in-memory-only, update-publish-placeholder, mac/linux-CONFIGURED-BUT-UNVERIFIED, es-ES-70%-sample). The doc-structure invariant is now fully mechanical: (1) top-of-guide preamble, (2) dedicated 'X trust floor — what the app does and doesn't promise' anchor with What-IS/What-IS-NOT, (3) inline 'Honesty reminder' callouts linking back to the anchor, (4) README front-door, (5) point-of-action UI (referenced not duplicated). The obligations are phase-specific; the structure never changes direction. Phase 7's twist vs prior phases: the obligations are about META-capabilities (how the app updates/measures/translates/ships) rather than document-editing fidelity — but the same five-surface ratchet applied without rediscovery.
fix: For a roadmap-CLOSING documentation wave: (a) write an honest 'Roadmap status' section in the README with a Phases-done summary + a documented-backlog TABLE (item / phase / what's pending) cross-checked against the reviewer's findings list — every HIGH/MEDIUM the final review left open must appear in the backlog table with its phase tag; (b) write an honest 'Platform support' table (VERIFIED vs CONFIGURED-BUT-UNVERIFIED) when cross-platform is config-only; (c) for any 'anonymous telemetry' feature, document the .strict()-schema structural-PII-guard as the developer-guide exemplar privacy pattern WITH the audit grep suite (rg for filePath|userId|content=0, fetch|http|sendBeacon=0, @sentry|posthog=0, .strict()>=1, console in record-handler=0) so the next project copies the pattern, not just the prose.

lesson: Cleared repo-wide CI lint debt (448 problems: 46 err + 402 warn) to 0/0 with ZERO runtime behavior change (1368/355 vitest count identical pre->post, same 13 failed files, no suite flipped). TWO non-obvious traps. (1) `eslint --fix` for @typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports AGGRESSIVELY mis-converts mixed value+type `import {}` statements into `import type {}`, silently breaking value usages (instanceof operands, `.of()` static calls, class-as-runtime-arg like lookupMaybe(x, PDFDict)). This passes lint GREEN but produces NEW TS1361/TS2206 typecheck errors absent from baseline. Hit 6 pdf-lib-importing files (form-engine, pades-detect, signature-appearance, field-dict-authoring, shape-annotations, pdfjs-source). (2) Pre-existing `eslint-disable-next-line` directives for multi-line `} as any` / useEffect-dep-array were MIS-POSITIONED: placed before the opening `{` so they disable the wrong line; the violation lands on the CLOSING line (`} as any` / `}, [deps])`). The disable must immediately precede the line ESLint attributes the violation to.
fix: After ANY `eslint --fix` run that could touch consistent-type-imports, ALWAYS run `npm run typecheck` (all 3 tsconfigs) before declaring done — lint-green != types-green. Repair over-conversions by splitting into `import { values }` + `import type { types }`; classify each symbol by grepping for value use (Sym., new Sym, instanceof Sym, Sym-as-bare-arg) vs type-only (annotation positions). For scoped disables on multi-line expressions, put `// eslint-disable-next-line` on the line directly above the node ESLint reports (closing `} as any`, the `}, [deps]` array line), NOT above the statement's opening. Verify by re-running `npm run lint`, not by eyeballing line numbers.

lesson: Could not reproduce Diego's Wave-29 '1718 passed / 5 failed' vitest baseline on this machine: 355 tests fail due to a better-sqlite3 native-ABI mismatch. The on-disk `better_sqlite3.node` is built for Electron's NODE_MODULE_VERSION 123 (via postinstall `electron-builder install-app-deps`), but vitest runs under local Node 24 = NODE_MODULE_VERSION 137. `npm rebuild better-sqlite3` (Node target) FAILS — better-sqlite3 11.10.0 has no Node-24 prebuild and node-gyp MSBuild compile errors under Node 24 headers; the failed rebuild also DELETED the existing .node, requiring `electron-rebuild -f -w better-sqlite3` to restore. This is a toolchain/env issue owned by Diego/Ravi, not a lint or behavior concern.
fix: For lint/refactor sweeps where you only need to prove 'no behavior change' (not 'all green'), establish your OWN pre-change baseline (count + set of failing suites), do the work, then prove the count AND the failed-suite SET are identical (set-diff both directions = empty). Don't chase a native-module rebuild that's another agent's domain. If a rebuild is unavoidable, note that a FAILED better-sqlite3 node-gyp build deletes the binary — restore with `npx electron-rebuild -f -w better-sqlite3` (Electron target) to leave the tree as found. Flag the ABI/Node-24 toolchain gap to Diego/Ravi.

task: Backlog-fix toolchain wave — better-sqlite3 Node-24 ABI skew + husky pre-commit
summary: Resolved the 6-wave recurring better-sqlite3 Node-24 ABI skew via Option A (Node-20 enforcement + a loud non-mutating guard) + Option C (non-destructive cache-prebuild escape hatch). Proven on this Node-24-only host: vitest run src/db = 350/350 PASS (was ~350 ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED); full npm test through the pretest guard = 1734 pass / 8 fail where the 8 are pre-existing non-ABI backlog/brittle tests (3 prod-render glyph David-domain + 5 renderer brittle Riley-domain). Added husky@9.1.7 + lint-staged@17.0.5 (both MIT) with a pre-commit hook (lint-staged + tsc -p tsconfig.main.json --noEmit safeguard) and a pre-push hook (full typecheck + lint). Proved the tsc safeguard catches David Wave-30 consistent-type-imports hazard (import type of a pdf-lib class used as value: ESLint exit 0 GREEN, tsc TS1361 RED -> hook blocks).
detail: This host has ONLY Node 24 (no Node 20, no nvm/fnm/volta), so Node 20 cannot be the RUNNING version here but IS enforced as the contract. engines.node tightened to >=20.10.0 <21 (emits loud npm warn EBADENGINE). scripts/check-node.mjs gates pretest --strict; it NEVER mutates node_modules. scripts/rebuild-native-for-node.mjs is the escape hatch: swaps the npm-cache prebuild matching the active Node ABI, verify-loads, backs up first, restores Electron-ABI with --electron. Deliberately did NOT set engine-strict=true (would block npm ci on Node 24 and strand the escape hatch). FOUND + FIXED a latent CI bug: ci.yml comment claimed CI rebuilt for the Node-20 ABI before tests but the check job only ran npm run rebuild (Electron ABI v123) which does NOT load under plain Node 20 (v115) -> db tests would ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED in CI too; replaced with npm rebuild better-sqlite3 in the check job (e2e+build keep electron rebuild).
key_learnings: When a recurring ABI skew has a KNOWN-dangerous remediation (from-source rebuild on Node 24: node-gyp 9.4.1 + Python 3.14 no-distutils + MSBuild exit 1 FAILS, and a prior failed rebuild DELETED the working binding), the durable fix is enforce-the-baseline + a NON-MUTATING guard + a non-destructive escape hatch — NEVER an auto-rebuild. The guard must diagnose and instruct, not compile.

There is no clean better-sqlite3@11.10.0 node-v137 prebuild anywhere; the only Node-24 binary in cache is a cross-major v12.9.0-node-v137 that works against the v11 JS shim for tests (350/350) but is a LOCAL-TEST convenience, never the shipped binary. Always restore the Electron-ABI binary before packaging.

A pre-commit lint hook MUST be paired with a tsc step: eslint --fix for @typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports collapses mixed value+type imports into import type, going lint-GREEN but tsc-RED (TS1361). Verified by repro. Scope the pre-commit tsc to the tsconfig where the hazard lives (main, where pdf-lib is imported) for speed; full typecheck on pre-push.

CI ABI targeting is per-JOB: the TEST job needs the plain-Node prebuild (npm rebuild better-sqlite3), the e2e/package jobs need the Electron-ABI binary (electron-builder install-app-deps). Node 20 ABI v115 != Electron 30 ABI v123 — a comment claiming they match was the false premise behind a latent CI bug.

Windows Node-spawned GNU tar (Git-for-Windows /usr/bin/tar) misparses an absolute C:\...\file.tar.gz archive as host:path. Prefer System32 bsdtar (handles drive letters) or pass GNU tar --force-local.
proposed_lock: L-003: Node 20 is the enforced local+CI baseline; check-node guard gates pretest; never from-source-rebuild better-sqlite3 on Node 24 (use rebuild:node escape hatch). Proposed to Marcus for ratification, not self-added.
recurrence: 6th+ time the ABI skew surfaced (Waves 13/21/24/25/28a/30); FIRST time it has a durable enforce+guard+escape-hatch resolution rather than a per-wave workaround.

task: Backlog-fix wave (backend): Fix1 image-export blank standard-font glyphs (v0.6.1 L-002), Fix2 quitAndInstall unsaved-work gate (H-29.1), Fix3 OCR catalog real SHA-256 (B-21.1)
summary: pdf.js-in-Node standard-font glyphs render BLANK when standardFontDataUrl is a file:// URL string, because (a) Node global fetch cannot read file:// URLs, and (b) pdf.js v4's NodeStandardFontDataFactory does fs.promises.readFile(url) on the VERBATIM file:// string (which is not a valid fs path) so the standard-14 fonts silently fail to load. The render does NOT throw — it just drops the glyphs — so a test asserting 'canvas not all-zero' PASSES on a white blank page. Fix: pass an explicit StandardFontDataFactory + CMapReaderFactory to getDocument that reads bytes via fs.readFile from the resolved absolute dir, removing the url-vs-path ambiguity and the isNodeJS-autodetect fragility. Also await page.getOperatorList() before render() to force font resolution ordering. Verified by dark-pixel-count regression: file:// url -> 0 dark px (blank); plain path OR factory -> 2628 dark px (text renders).
patterns: pdf.js-in-Node font loading: do NOT rely on standardFontDataUrl as a file:// URL — Node fetch can't read file:// and pdf.js v4's Node factory fs.readFile's the verbatim url string. Supply an explicit StandardFontDataFactory/CMapReaderFactory that fs.readFile's from an absolute dir.

Image/render regression tests must assert ASSET PRESENCE (glyph dark-pixel count vs a blank baseline), NOT just 'no throw + non-blank canvas' — a white blank page is non-blank (all 255s). Confirms Diego's 2026-05-27 global learning.

Latent data-loss gate (H-29.1): mirror the existing confirm-required pattern (OCR signed_pdf_requires_confirm) — add a request boolean (confirmedDiscardUnsaved) + a typed error variant (unsaved_work_blocks_install) + an injected probe; enforce in main, drive the dialog from the renderer.

OCR language packs have TWO byte sources: bundled eng comes from npm @tesseract.js-data/eng@4.0.0 (10.9MB, hash-verified-once), downloadable packs come from the 4.0.0_fast CDN (1-2MB gz). Compute download SHAs over the EXACT CDN gz bytes the manager fetches, not the npm artifact.
evidence: npx vitest run src/main/export 80/80 (incl 3 new font-glyph regressions Helvetica/Times/Courier + 2 prior); src/main+src/ipc+src/preload 830/830; tsc main/preload/renderer clean; eslint 0/0 on touched files.
outcome_detail: All 3 fixes shipped + tested. Handoffs: Riley owns the renderer 'Save before updating?' dialog + errors.json i18n string for unsaved_work_blocks_install; Diego owns the optional release-time scripts/build-ocr-catalog.mjs.

task: Backlog-Fix wave — Phase 7 28c deep modal-step i18n extraction + annotation-layer a11y + David quitAndInstall dialog handoff (PDF_Viewer_Editor)
detail: Fix 1: swept 25 deep modal-step components (PAdES/signature-capture/OCR/export/mail-merge/image-import/language-pack/scan/save-template/help/export-engine) to t(); en-US baseline 482->816 keys (+334), es-ES 70%->68% held at trust-floor sample. Fix 2: annotation-layer pointer-only drawing div got role=application + t()-routed aria-label naming the keyboard alternative (toolbar+Inspector); ORIGINAL no-static-element-interactions disable REMOVED; ONE scoped no-noninteractive-element-interactions disable remains. Also wired David's quitAndInstall Save-before-updating dialog. tsc + eslint src/client clean; 585 pass/5 fail (all 5 pre-existing env-skew). Non-obvious takeaways: (1) DEFERRED-A11Y-ON-POINTER-ONLY-SURFACES IS A RECURRING TRAP. A pointer-driven canvas/drawing div triggers a CASCADE of jsx-a11y rules, not one: removing the role-less state fixes no-static-element-interactions but then role=application trips no-noninteractive-element-interactions AND no-noninteractive-tabindex, because jsx-a11y's role taxonomy lists the WAI-ARIA `application` role (which is SEMANTICALLY interactive per spec) as non-interactive. There is NO disable-free path with the correct role unless you own the eslint config (roles allowlist). The honest outcome for a genuine drawing surface is role+accessible-name (a real win over a nameless div) + ONE narrowly-justified disable for the taxonomy false-positive. Don't chase a zero-disable on pointer-only widgets — that's how the original got deferred. (2) eslint-disable-next-line covers ONLY the literally-next source line; on a multi-line JSX element, a rule that reports at an ATTRIBUTE line (e.g. no-noninteractive-tabindex on the tabIndex= line) is NOT suppressed by a directive above the element's opening line. Multi-rule single-line disables only help if both rules report at that same next line. (3) When extracting i18n from a component whose tool/enum union names DON'T match the JSON keys (annotation tool 'text'/'strikeout'/'ink' vs toolbar keys textBox/strikethrough/freehand), a dynamic t(`ns:${value}`) silently produces missing keys (resolveKey returns the bare path) AND defeats the typed-key gate — use an explicit Record<Union,key> map instead. (4) Grep for 'useT' to find swept components gives FALSE POSITIVES: 'useState'/'useTsa'/'useTablistKeys' all contain the substring; verify with `import.*useT.*from` to find genuine consumers. (5) Pre-existing test failures masquerade as your regressions in a big string sweep — triage by reading each error: setCert auto-advancing padesStep, RTL unmount+rerender, jsdom blob.arrayBuffer gap, and getByText multi-match are all structural/env, NOT string-driven. Only 2 of 7 were mine (tab label /Drawn/->/Draw/).
fix: For any deferred-a11y-on-pointer-only-surface item: expect a rule cascade, give the surface role=application + a t()-routed accessible name that documents the keyboard alternative, remove the ORIGINAL disable, and accept ONE justified disable for the application-role taxonomy false positive (note that re-classifying the role belongs in the eslint config owner's domain). For i18n union->key mismatches use an explicit Record map, never dynamic template-literal keys. Measure extracted-key count with coverage.test (816 actual), never trust the design estimate.

task: Backlog-Fix wave review (David glyph/quitAndInstall/catalog + Riley 28c-i18n/annotation-a11y + Diego toolchain), parallel with Diego v0.7.1 repack
verdict: GREEN — all 5 fixes close their findings; v0.7.1 can ship. 1 HIGH found+fixed by me (H-FIX.1), 0 MEDIUM, 3 LOW, 1 NIT.
lesson: LATENT-GATE FIXES CAN THEMSELVES BE LATENT. H-29.1's quitAndInstall unsaved-work gate had a textbook-correct, fully-test-proven MAIN-side controller (ordering AFTER configured+version guards, BEFORE the irreversible quit; refuses on unsaved+unconfirmed; 5 branches pinned) — yet the END-TO-END behavior was still broken: production wires hasUnsavedWork:()=>false (dirty state is renderer-owned, mirroring app:quit's hasUnsavedChanges:()=>false), so the main gate's blocking branch NEVER fires in prod, making the renderer's confirm dialog (gated solely on outcome.kind==='blocked-unsaved') dead code. A user with unsaved work clicking 'Restart and install' would still lose work without a prompt — the same data-loss bug, one layer down. Also re-verified the David<->Diego font-path packaged-binary seam (the L-002 dev-works/packaged-breaks class): David's resolveExportFontData require.resolve('pdfjs-dist/package.json')->standard_fonts/cmaps EXACTLY matches Diego's electron-builder.yml asarUnpack of those 3 paths, and EACH file comments the other's load-bearing line — seam aligned, no flag needed.
fix: (1) When a gate/auth/permission check's state is 'renderer-owned' and the main-side probe is defensive-FALSE, the RENDERER must drive the check against its OWN authoritative signal (here selectIsDirty, which UpdateStatusArea already had store access to) BEFORE invoking the irreversible action; the main gate is defense-in-depth, never the live trigger. Don't stop the audit at 'the gate refuses when told there's unsaved work' — trace whether anything in production ever TELLS it. A test-green gate wired to a constant-false probe is still broken. (2) When a thunk returns void and silently swallows a cancel (saveDocumentThunk swallows a cancelled Save-As), re-read FRESH store state after awaiting it (the closure-captured selector value is stale) and fail-safe — re-open the confirm rather than proceed with the destructive action. (3) Adding a new cross-slice selector to a WIDELY-RENDERED shared component breaks every partial test store that omits that slice — grep for all test stores mounting the component and add the reducer (about-modal + settings-phase7 stores both omitted the document slice the shared UpdateStatusArea now reads). (4) For a packaged-binary asset-path contract shared by two agents, REQUIRE each file to comment the other's exact resolution mechanism — that mutual cross-reference is what prevented the L-002 class here.
ratchets: 7th consecutive clean wave; verified 0 new as-any (the as-typeof-NodePath/NodeUrl casts are typed-namespace casts via type-only imports, NOT as-any), the new require('node:path'/'node:url') are built-ins following the existing export-bootstrap idiom (never vite-bundled), pdfjs-dist stays behind the indirect dynamic-import dodge; 0 sentinels (B-21.1 sentinel ELIMINATED — all 10 catalog SHAs real distinct 64-hex); 0 TODO/FIXME in changed files.
evidence: Re-ran (not trusted): vitest src/main/export 132 PASS incl REAL @napi-rs/canvas prod-render glyph regression (NOT skipped); src/main+ipc+preload 830 PASS; src/client/i18n+update-handlers 71 PASS; full src/client 585 PASS/5 pre-existing brittle fail (Riley baseline reproduced exactly after my test-store repair); tsc clean x3; eslint 0/0 all changed files; i18n 816 en/558 es keys, 0 dup/orphan/empty.
residuals: L-FIX.1 prod-render reimplements the font factory rather than importing production resolveExportFontData (could drift)
L-FIX.2 install-confirm dialog has no component test (data-loss UI, regression-coverage anti-pattern)
L-FIX.3 inline alertdialog lacks aria-modal/own focus-trap (mitigated by parent modal)
N-FIX.1 no visual screenshot — Diego v0.7.1 repack must capture L-002 shot + exercise the install-confirm path for H-FIX.1 pixel confirmation

task: v0.7.1 repack — verify David font-path<->asarUnpack seam, clean build, package NSIS+portable, L-002 PNG-text + es-ES visual proof
summary: Verified David's Phase-6.2 image-export glyph fix resolves IN THE PACKAGED BINARY. The load-bearing seam is export-bootstrap.ts:resolveExportFontData()+makeNodeDataFactory() which fs.readFile()s font/cmap bytes from dirname(require.resolve('pdfjs-dist/package.json'))/standard_fonts — an ABSOLUTE path (NOT the broken file://-URL-string that caused v0.6.1 blank text). My electron-builder.yml asarUnpack unpacks pdfjs-dist/{package.json,standard_fonts,cmaps}; Electron's asar shim redirects require.resolve to app.asar.unpacked BECAUSE package.json is unpacked, so the sibling font dirs resolve as real files. VERDICT: seam ALIGNED, no config change. PROVEN empirically: rendered a 150dpi Helvetica PDF through the SHIPPED engine against the SHIPPED app.asar.unpacked tree -> 25688 dark pixels (v0.6.1 produced 0/blank), visually confirmed crisp glyphs. v0.7.1 NSIS+portable packaged. Test count IMPROVED 1734->1765 pass (David's 3 glyph tests + Riley's 3 string tests now green; remaining 5 are Riley's pre-existing brittle renderer tests). es-ES locale switch flips full UI to Spanish in packaged binary (Configuracion dialog, menu strip, telemetry/locale honesty copy). docx+xlsx export still valid.
key_learnings: A font/asset seam between two agents (David's fs.readFile path-construction <-> my asarUnpack globs) cannot be verified by reading code or by unit tests alone — both passed while v0.6.1 shipped blank text. The decisive check is rendering through the SHIPPED engine against the SHIPPED app.asar.unpacked tree and COUNTING INK PIXELS in the output (25688 vs 0). This is the export-side analogue of the L-002 launch screenshot: pixels-on-disk, not handles.

The asar-shim require.resolve redirect is conditional: Electron redirects a path to app.asar.unpacked/ ONLY when THAT file is itself unpacked. Unpacking standard_fonts/cmaps WITHOUT also unpacking package.json would leave require.resolve('pdfjs-dist/package.json') pointing inside the asar, so dirname()+join('standard_fonts') would point INTO the asar and fs.readFile would ENOENT. The package.json unpack (electron-builder.yml:138) is load-bearing, not incidental.

To exercise production .ts engine code (export-bootstrap.ts) from a standalone evidence script under tsx on Node 24: the source uses require()+top-level patterns that conflict with an ESM (.mjs) entry -> ERR_AMBIGUOUS_MODULE_SYNTAX. Use a .cts entry so tsx treats the whole import graph as CommonJS; require() works natively and dynamic import() still loads ESM-only deps (pdf-lib). A --tsconfig module:commonjs override does NOT fix it because MY entry's .mjs extension still forces ESM on the graph.

Settings persist across packaged launches (deleteAppDataOnUninstall:false) — a prior es-ES write means the next launch shot is ALREADY in Spanish. Useful (the launch shot doubles as a full-chrome locale proof) but note it in the report so es-ES isn't mistaken for the default.

Verifying the packaged better-sqlite3 is Electron-ABI: the WORKING-TREE binary should THROW ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED under plain Node 24 — that throw is the PASS signal (it's the Electron-v123 prebuild, packaging-correct), not a failure. Confirm the packaged copy by size (1720320 B = v11.10.0 electron-v123) and by a clean Playwright _electron launch (0 page errors).
recurrence: First v0.7.1 repack; the font-path seam class first surfaced as the v0.6.1 L-002 blank-text bug — now closed with packaged-binary pixel proof.

task: Backlog-fix v0.7.1 documentation refresh (PDF_Viewer_Editor)
detail: Refreshed README + user-guide + developer-guide + api-reference for v0.7.1. Moved 7 resolved backlog items to a Resolved table and isolated the 3 genuinely-blocked items (mac/linux real-hardware, real publish-target+signing-cert, native WIA/TWAIN binding) into a clearly-labeled "Deferred — requires external resources" section with one-line external-resource reasons each. The load-bearing discipline this wave: documented the H-FIX.1 unsaved-work install gate as correct-in-source but NOT active-in-the-0.7.1-binary (binary predates the renderer wire; install path unreachable until a real publish target exists; functional impact zero; self-resolves next packaging pass). This binary-vs-source honesty is trust-floor-adjacent — never claim a fix ships in the binary when only the source has it.
