PowerShell Add-Type -MemberDefinition + -UsingNamespace duplicates the using directive into the generated C# source — use -TypeDefinition with explicit usings + full class declaration for clean compile on PS 5.1 + 7.x

summary: Closed Julian's Phase 7.2 §8 deferred finding 7.2.3 — Vite define-fold of process.env.NODE_ENV -> "production" in electron.vite.config.ts (function-form defineConfig, scoped to mode==='production', applied to main + preload blocks). Result: zero ipcMain.handle calls for __test:whichBridge or __test:seedOcrJob in dist/main/index.js (prod build); registerTestWhichBridge fully tree-shaken; registerTestSeedOcrJob collapsed to an empty function shell. Combined main+preload bundle delta -4,710 bytes (-4.6 KB). e2e GREEN against the new npm run build:test bundle (Phase A-E total 4482ms; bridge probe still asserts all six slots = sqlite). Locked as L-006.
pattern: Two-bundle build for security-gated test channels. Default `build` script bakes the prod-mode define-fold (DCE drops registration); new `build:test` script (electron-vite build --mode test) bypasses the fold for the Playwright harness. CI's e2e job switched to build:test; packaging job (dist:win) keeps default build. The single config helper prodNodeEnvDefine(mode) is the only place the rule lives.
takeaways: Vite/Rollup `define` matches dot-syntax (process.env.NODE_ENV) at the AST identifier-access chain level — it does NOT match bracket-syntax (process.env['NODE_ENV']) which is a computed-string property-access AST. With noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true (this repo's tsconfig), both forms return string|undefined so dot is type-equivalent — there is no type-safety reason to prefer bracket. Always normalize to dot at gate sites that are meant to be statically foldable.

electron-vite's defineConfig accepts a function `({command, mode}) => ElectronViteConfig`. `electron-vite build` defaults to mode='production'; `electron-vite dev` runs command='serve' with mode='development'. `--mode <name>` is the official CLI flag for naming a custom build mode like 'test'.

When you scope a define to mode==='production', e2e harnesses that need the runtime gate to keep working MUST build via a non-prod mode. The split-bundle pattern (build vs build:test) is cleaner than per-mode runtime branches.

lint-staged config that uses `*.{ts,tsx}` (bare glob) will feed `.eslintrc.cjs`-ignored files (like electron.vite.config.ts) to ESLint, which emits a 'File ignored' WARNING that trips `--max-warnings 0` and blocks the commit. The robust fix on ESLint 8.x (which lacks --no-warn-ignored, that's a v9 flag) is to narrow the lint-staged glob to match the ignore list exactly — e.g. `src/**/*.{ts,tsx}` + `tests/**/*.ts`, mirroring the `npm run lint` script.

Rollup's DCE leaves an empty function shell `function fn() { return; }` when only the early-return survives after a constant-folded conditional, but DCEs the function entirely when its callee chain (e.g. getDbBridgeKinds in this case) is unreferenced elsewhere. The asymmetry between registerTestSeedOcrJob (shell kept) and registerTestWhichBridge (fully gone) is normal — depends on whether the deps closure has any other consumer in the graph.

task: Phase 7.2 7.2.4 closure — signed-PDF + OCR invalidation back-ref e2e
detail: Three changes shipped on 8e32b0c: (1) deterministic signed-PDF fixture under tests/fixtures/pdfs/signed-1p-eng.pdf (125842 bytes, sha=bac979218bfe0aac...) generated by a new sibling generate-signed-fixture.mjs against a committed test-only PFX (test-signing.pfx + PRNG_SEED). Determinism via fixed-epoch Date shim covering plainAddPlaceholder + signpdf.sign + a seeded forge.random.getBytes override neutralizing RSA blinding. (2) Two new test-only IPC channels __test:seedSignatureAudit + __test:listSignatureAudit with the same registration-time dot-syntax NODE_ENV==='test' gate as the existing __test:* channels. Drive-by extended SignatureAuditRowDto + RaviSignatureAuditRow + memory-repo + adapter to surface invalidatedByOcrJobId (Phase-5 SELECT already projected it; bridge DTO hadn't yet). (3) New e2e spec tests/e2e/signed-pdf-ocr-invalidation.spec.ts seeds the audit row, runs production runOnDocument with invalidatesSignaturesConfirmed:true, asserts invalidatedByOcrJobId null->newJobId. Verified: typecheck 3-tsconfig GREEN, lint --max-warnings 0 GREEN, vitest 1951/1951 in 21.76s, new e2e 3.8s wall-clock, full e2e suite 11.3s. Prod-bundle L-006 grep confirms zero handler-body leakage + only inert Channels enum strings + empty registerTest* shells.
takeaways: When making a signed-PDF fixture byte-deterministic, monkey-patching globalThis.Date is necessary BUT NOT SUFFICIENT — node-signpdf's plainAddPlaceholder ALSO calls new Date() (for the sig dict's /M modification-date entry, via the pdfkitAddPlaceholder helper) and forge.random.getBytes is consumed during RSA blinding. The full recipe: fixed-epoch Date shim around both plainAddPlaceholder() AND signpdf.sign(), PLUS forge.random.getBytes/getBytesSync override with a seeded PRNG. Verified deterministic across 3 separate node-process invocations once both shims layered together; one shim alone was insufficient (and produced confusing 'first call hash != subsequent calls' patterns that initially looked like module-state bugs).

node-signpdf v3's plainAddPlaceholder writes /V as an INDIRECT REFERENCE (PDFRef -> object stream entry). The production detector at src/main/pdf-ops/pades-detect.ts only handles inline /V (PDFDict), so a real PAdES-signed PDF trips a silent no-match. The OCR test at ocr-run-on-document.test.ts:250 hand-authors /V as an inline dict via doc.context.obj({...}), masking this. Worked around in the fixture by post-processing: load with pdf-lib, resolve the indirect /V via doc.context.lookup(v, PDFDict), set it back inline, save again. The byte-range becomes stale (offsets shift on re-serialize) but the detection + invalidation paths work; the e2e doesn't verify the signature cryptographically. PROBABLY a latent bug in pades-detect.ts for any real-world PAdES-signed PDF from Acrobat / DocuSign / Adobe Sign — flagged for Julian / David follow-up but out of scope for this single-finding closure.

The production OCR handler uses the file-hash algorithm (SHA-256 of first 64 KiB || ASCII size string, src/main/pdf-ops/file-hash.ts) as docHash — NOT a raw SHA-256 of the full file. E2e tests that seed audit rows keyed by docHash must mirror that algorithm exactly; I lost a run to assuming raw SHA-256 and got fileHash=8c874e85... vs fixture-bytes-hash=bac97921.... The fixture-hash gate (expected-hashes.json) IS raw SHA-256 because verify-hashes.mjs is a separate concern from per-document hashing. Two different SHA-256 algorithms, two different consumers, easy to confuse.

Surgical bridge-DTO extension is a low-risk drive-by — adding invalidatedByOcrJobId to SignatureAuditRowDto + RaviSignatureAuditRow + adapter toDto + memory repo took ~30 lines and zero existing-caller breakage because: (a) the SQL SELECT already projected the column (Ravi's Wave-16 work), (b) the production read path didn't filter on it, (c) the memory repo was already a no-op for mark, so making it an actual mutating mark INCREASED its fidelity to SQLite rather than breaking any test. The L-006 dot-syntax channel-gate pattern from Phase 7.2.3 (Diego's prior entry) carried over verbatim for the new two channels — recipe is solid, just copy it.

task: Close Dependabot CVE-2026-47429 (vitest critical)
detail: Bumped vitest 2.1.9 -> 3.2.6 + @vitest/ui 2.1.9 -> 3.2.6 in package.json + lockfile regen (5 packages added, 13 changed transitively). Closes Dependabot alerts #3 (package-lock.json) and #4 (package.json) — both critical CVSS 9.8 against CVE-2026-47429 / GHSA-5xrq-8626-4rwp (Vitest UI server arbitrary file read + RCE via Windows path-shape \?\..\ bypassing isFileServingAllowed). Project exposure was low (devDep CLI runner, no --api.host invocations) but the bump closes the door on accidental future exposure. License unchanged (MIT). v2->v3 had ZERO migration fixes needed on this 1951-test suite (vi.mock / vi.hoisted / fake-timers / snapshot serializers all compatible at call-sites used). L-006 grep clean post-bump. CI run 27298733059 GREEN at 6m59s on push. v4 deferred to a separate dispatch.
takeaways: vitest 2.x -> 3.x is a near-zero-migration major bump for typical Vitest harness patterns (vi.mock, vi.hoisted, fake-timers, snapshot serializers). The repo's 1951-test suite migrated transparently. Don't fear the v2->v3 jump for security-driven bumps unless the harness uses browser mode or advanced workspace config.

When a Dependabot alert fires against both package.json AND package-lock.json for the same underlying vuln, both close on a single lockfile-bearing commit — they index separately but share a fix. Don't over-engineer with two separate PRs.

Smallest-major bump strategy for critical-severity CVEs: pick the lowest version >= advisory's first_patched_version, NOT the latest major. Latest is tempting but adds unrelated breaking-change exposure; lowest patched is the minimum-blast-radius fix.
Dependabot's auto-dismissal lags the lockfile-bearing commit by minutes to hours (next scan cycle); the work is done when the commit lands + CI is green, not when the alert flips state.

task: Phase 7.2 7.2.5 — fix pades-detect.ts indirect /V handling + drop fixture workaround + 7.2.6 cross-ref comment
summary: Routed every dict-typed read in pades-detect.ts (/FT, /V, /Contents) through pdf-lib's dict.lookupMaybe(name, Type) which transparently resolves PDFRefs. Added 4 new tests in pades-detect.test.ts (inline baseline, indirect /V, indirect /V+/Contents, indirect-empty-placeholder negative). Removed inlineSignatureDict post-pass from generate-signed-fixture.mjs (-16235 bytes, sha256 bac97921 -> f60bc14c); /ByteRange now intact. signed-pdf-ocr-invalidation.spec.ts e2e GREEN in 4.7s against the indirect-ref fixture — production wiring now correct end-to-end. Drive-by: cross-ref comments file-hash.ts <-> verify-hashes.mjs distinguishing the two hash algorithms (per Diego's 2026-06-10 L3 takeaway).
takeaways: pdf-lib's `dict.lookupMaybe(name, Type)` is the right primitive for ANY PDF dict read where the value might be indirect — it resolves PDFRefs AND type-guards in one call, with no-throw semantics on type mismatch. Use it as the default; raw `dict.get` is only safe for values guaranteed inline by your own authoring code.

Cache-pooled PDFName.of() means referential equality (`ft === PDFName.of('Sig')`) is correct AND cleaner than `String(ft).includes('Sig')`. The latter risks false-positive matches on /SigFlags / /Signature.

Test-only inline-shape fixtures are a silent-no-match risk class. When the production-shape differs from the hand-authored test shape (here: indirect-ref /V vs inline-dict /V), the unit suite passes while production is broken. Cross-check: does my test author the data the way the production caller will see it after a real-world tool produces the file? The e2e exposed this; the unit suite never would have.

Diego's 2026-06-10 'inlineSignatureDict' workaround is a textbook example of a workaround whose root cause is fixable upstream. The post-pass invalidates the byte-range (re-serialize shifts offsets) — a side effect that was acceptable for the e2e but inconsistent for any future signature-verify. Fixing the root cause restores the byte-range AND removes 30 lines of fixture-script code.

On Node 24 dev hosts, switching between Node-ABI and Electron-ABI better-sqlite3 bindings is the OBVIOUS step you'll forget. After `node scripts/rebuild-native-for-node.mjs` (for vitest) you MUST `node scripts/rebuild-native-for-node.mjs --electron` before running playwright — otherwise the bridge fails with `bridge_not_initialized` and the e2e looks broken when it's a tooling skew.

summary: Added launchPackagedApp helper + packaged-smoke spec; spec passes against release/smoke-v0.7.20/win-unpacked/ in 2.3s. Closes the Phase 7.3 candidate filed in the v0.7.20 release-ceremony JSONL entry.
pattern: For e2e harnesses that need to span dev-launch and prod-launch shapes against an Electron app where test-only IPC channels are DCE'd from prod bundles (L-006 here): the cleanest cut is to design the prod-launch smoke spec to depend ONLY on the production surface (always-mounted bridge methods, empty-state DOM, zero console errors) — NOT to try to teach the helper to surface test channels through a separate test-mode-built artifact. The prod-bundle smoke gives you one always-runnable signal against the standard release-ceremony artifact, and the test-mode-artifact path can be reserved for ad-hoc bonus runs that don't sit on the CI critical path.
takeaways: Playwright's _electron.launch({executablePath, args}) accepts the same Chromium switches as the args:['.'] dev-launch shape — --user-data-dir=<dir> as the first positional arg works identically against a packaged exe and a `.` repo-root launch.
Discovery via fs.readdirSync + mtime sort over release/smoke-v*/win-unpacked/ is more robust than hardcoding the current version; it auto-tracks each new smoke ceremony.

Defensive tessdata seeding into userData is cheap (<50ms) and protects against build-mode drift in extraResources evaluation. Worth doing even when the packaged binary normally ships bundled tessdata.
CI gating for packaged-binary specs needs a post-build job that consumes the windows-installers artifact — not an e2e-job addition. The dependency direction matters.

task: Acrobat parity + tool-marking audit (research only, no code changes)
summary: Audit shipped at docs/acrobat-parity-audit.md (588 lines, ~30 KB). 62 Acrobat tools surveyed: 30 ✅ / 8 ⚠️ / 17 ❌ / 3 🚫 cloud / 4 ➕ unique. 44 of our tools marked-scored across 5 dimensions: 31 fully marked / 6 partial / 7 gaps. Top finding: stale 'Coming in Phase N' tooltips for SHIPPED phases (Find/Fill&Sign/Shapes) are the worst marking failure. Proposed a tool-registry pattern + 4 Vitest enforcement tests (one specifically catches stale-phase tooltips) as a future L-007 lock candidate.
takeaways: Marking failures live in i18n: hardcoded English in shape-toolbar.tsx (eight labels + container ARIA) slipped past the Wave-29 Julian audit because the grep didn't visit src/client/components/shape-tools/ — recommend extending the §8 grep to subdirectories.

Menu<->toolbar drift is structural: 9 annotation+page-op toolbar buttons have no menu mirror. The fix is structural (tool-registry as single source of truth) not tactical (don't manually add 9 menu items).

Roadmap drift shows up in the user UI: 'Coming in Phase 4' tooltips persist after Phase 4 ships because no test asserts otherwise. The §5.3 stale-tooltip test would have caught Find/Fill&Sign/Shapes the day Phase 3/4 closed.

summary: Shipped three focused commits closing Bucket A quick wins from the Acrobat parity audit. A1 (b251546): refreshed 7 stale Phase-N tooltips + killed all dishonest 'coming later' toasts (Find / Fill&Sign / Shapes / Scan tooltip / Insert Blank menu / Insert from file toolbar / Page from File menu + bonus settings:themeComingSoon). A2 (7d26c45): i18n-wrapped 17 hardcoded English strings in shape-toolbar.tsx (8 labels + 8 ARIAs + container ARIA) under toolbar:shapeTools.* — added 25 en-US keys + matching es-ES initial translations. A4 (d7d4846): added 9 menu mirrors (Edit > Rotate CW/CCW/Delete Page + Tools > Highlight/Sticky/TextBox/Underline/Strikethrough/Freehand), each dispatching the SAME action as the toolbar button. 13 new tests, 659 / 659 green. Wired Fill & Sign menu to the existing openCaptureModal() action — making it the first top-level entry point for the shipped signature-capture flow.
takeaways: The Wave-29 Julian a11y audit grep didn't visit src/client/components/shape-tools/ — that's how 17 hardcoded English strings + zero i18n in a Phase-4-deliverable component slipped through. Future a11y grep ratchets should walk EVERY src/client/components/ subtree, not just the ones the auditor remembers.

The phase3() / 'coming later' toast pattern was an early-phase shortcut that ages badly. If a feature has a real-but-deferred dispatcher, prefer 'visible-but-disabled with honest tooltip' over 'looks enabled then toasts on click'. The latter is a discoverability lie; the former is a documented gap.

menu-bar tests must include EVERY reducer their MenuBar reads selectors from — partial stores silently throw at first selector evaluation (TypeError reading 'currentPage'). The fix is mechanical (add the reducer) but the failure mode is non-obvious if the new selector is added in a different commit than the test-store extension.

ShapeToolbar component is built + tested + i18n-clean but is NOT mounted in the production renderer. Worth a follow-up dispatch to wire it into the toolbar's Shapes button (which today is disabled + honest about the deferral).

openCaptureModal() in signatures-slice was a fully-shipped action with zero production UI callers — discovered while wiring Fill & Sign. Many flows shipped through Phase 4 without a top-level menu/toolbar entry; menu-mirror waves like A4 also surface 'where DO users start this workflow?' gaps.

summary: A5: mounted ShapeToolbar via FormDesignerToolbar pattern — new ui.shapesPanelOpen flag drives sub-toolbar visibility, main toolbar Shapes button is now an aria-pressed toggle, Esc inside sub-toolbar closes it (container-scoped handler, leaves global modal-Esc intact), first-button focus on open. A6: added Fill & Sign toolbar button in Forms group with new pen-signature icon dispatching openCaptureModal (same as Tools menu mirror from A4). i18n complete in en-US + es-ES for both. Two sequential commits 25d0adb + 28bfb27, pushed to main.
takeaways: When repurposing an i18n key that a test pinned as the canonical proof-locale-gap sample (resolve.test.ts used toolbar:shapes), update the test's anchor key to one that remains untranslated — toolbar:insertFromFile was the natural successor and stays untranslated until that feature ships.

FormDesignerToolbar set a reusable pattern for sub-toolbar reveal: ui-slice boolean + container component that returns null on closed + sibling mount under main Toolbar. Reused verbatim for ShapeToolbar, no architecture refactor needed. This pattern should be the default for future sub-toolbars (annotation properties bar, redaction sub-tools).

TOOLBAR_BUTTON_COUNT is a load-bearing constant for the roving-tabindex allocator — adding a button silently mis-aligns the focus map unless this is bumped. Already documented in-code; flagged here so future Riley waves don't miss it.

task: Phase 7.4 B1 Redaction design (Wave 1)
detail: Picked R1 (rasterize-redact) over R2 (content-stream walker) for v1 — correctness-first; reuses Phase 5 rasterize pipeline; trust-floor warning surfaced for searchability loss. Spec'd one IPC channel (pdf:applyRedactions), 17-row sanitize matrix reusing stripDocLevelJavaScript, PAdES gate mirroring ocr-run-on-document's invalidatesSignaturesConfirmed discipline + new nullable signature_audit_log.invalidated_by_redaction_at column (migration v8), and a 23-test unit catalog including the non-negotiable U-1 'text-extract over redacted rect MUST be empty' invariant. File-ownership matrix has zero overlaps across David/Ravi/Riley.
takeaways: combine.ts's JS-strip-by-construction property does NOT extend to R1 redaction (R1 is raster-replace via PDFDocument.load, not copyPages), so the JS strip must be EXPLICIT — David must call stripDocLevelJavaScript on the loaded doc before serialize.

Phase 5's signature_audit_log.invalidated_by_ocr_job_id is an FK to a long-running job; redaction has no equivalent lifecycle (single sync IPC). Picked a nullable timestamp invalidated_by_redaction_at instead of a parallel redaction_jobs table — minimal schema delta, upgradeable to FK if async redaction lands.

R2 (content-stream walker) deferral isn't blocked by pdf-lib's PDFContentStream.parse() (which IS accessible) — it's blocked by the test corpus to prove correctness across CID fonts + ligatures + rotated runs. R1 punts this complexity at the cost of searchability.

name: Ravi
summary: Migration 0008 (nullable invalidated_by_redaction_at column + partial index on signature_audit_log) + repo method markInvalidatedByRedaction(docHash, fieldNames) returning rows-affected, using a per-arity prepared-statement cache for the WHERE field_name IN (...) clause. SignatureAuditRow extended. 12 new tests + 2 extended schema tests; signature-audit-repo file at 64 tests GREEN; full suite 1984/1984. Pushed on commit 9a9d769.
takeaways: The orchestrator brief mentioned a COALESCE on a generic 'invalidated_at' column that doesn't exist in this schema — followed Riley's actual design (single column invalidated_by_redaction_at, always stamped) and flagged the discrepancy.

Riley's existing markInvalidatedByOcrJob takes (rowIds, ocrJobId) at the repo layer while the bridge does the docHash+fieldNames -> rowIds translation. The new markInvalidatedByRedaction moves that translation into SQL (doc_hash + field_name IN (?)), keeping the IPC handler signature simple. Different pattern; same intent.

better-sqlite3 prepared statements cannot bind an array to a single ? for IN-list — must expand to N placeholders. Cache keyed by arity gives O(1) re-prep cost for repeated apply with same arity (production case: 1-10 signed fields per doc, lots of reuse).
Parallel-wave commit hygiene: pre-push hook runs typecheck against the WHOLE working tree, not just staged files. Stashing other-owner WIP before push, restoring after, is the canonical pattern.

name: David
task: Phase 7.4 B1 Wave 2 — R1 redact engine + IPC handler + db-bridge mark
detail: R1 rasterize-redact engine + pdf:applyRedactions handler + signature-audit markInvalidatedByRedaction bridge. 25 engine + 23 handler + 1 new bridge integration tests; full suite 2067/2067. Commit 1078669.
takeaways: Sanitize via REBUILD-FROM-SCRATCH (PDFDocument.create + copyPages) is strictly stronger than catalog.delete() + save() — pdf-lib emits every object in context.indirectObjects regardless of reachability, so catalog.delete leaves orphan dicts (JS action bodies, /Info dicts, removed-page /Annots) in the output xref. The combine.ts strip-by-construction property DOES extend to redaction once you adopt the same rebuild pattern. Riley's §4.3 warning about combine NOT extending was correct for the load-+-mutate path; the rebuild path side-steps it entirely.

pdf-lib quirk: PDFDocument.load(bytes) without {updateMetadata: false} calls updateInfoDict() at constructor time which OVERWRITES Producer to 'pdf-lib (...)'. setProducer() sets the field but the SUBSEQUENT load-pass (e.g. in a test assertion) re-overwrites it. The actual bytes carry your Producer; assertion code must load with {updateMetadata: false} to read it back.

Structural test probes for 'page content is image-only' beat substring-byte probes for compressed content streams. pdf-lib content streams are FlateDecode-compressed by default; bytesIncludeAscii(output, TOKEN) returns false even when the token is logically still present. Instead assert: page has /XObject /Image resource AND no /Font resource — that's the structural definition of a raster-only page.
fix: For future content-stream-mutating ops on pdf-lib docs: (a) prefer rebuild-from-scratch over load-+-delete-+-save; (b) when load+save is required, the only reliable Producer override is to bypass pdf-lib via direct trailerInfo.Info reassignment + load the assertion-side doc with {updateMetadata: false}; (c) for assertions on R1-style raster pages, probe page.node Resources for /XObject /Image + absence of /Font rather than searching the serialized byte stream.

name: Riley
summary: Phase 7.4 B1 Wave 2 renderer surface: redactions slice + components/redaction-tools/{toolbar, overlay, apply-modal} + Apply thunk with two-pass PAdES confirm + i18n bundles (en-US + es-ES) + menu mirrors + toolbar Redact button + shortcuts. 47 new tests; full vitest src/client/ 697 green; typecheck + lint green. Commit 9d9f731 pushed. Renderer feature-detects window.pdfApi.pdf.applyRedactions so the build is green even before David's preload registration lands.
key_lessons: Mid-wave coordination pattern: when parallel agents land work to the same files in tandem, identify other agent's uncommitted-and-partial work via git status, stash it with self-describing labels, commit own work, restore via stash pop. This protects against own commit blocking on the partial peer state.

Renderer feature-detect for not-yet-landed peer IPC contract: route through window.pdfApi.pdf as any with a function-type guard and return Result<...,'engine_failed'> on the bridge-not-yet-exposed branch. Keeps the renderer build green today AND routes through to David's real bridge the moment his preload lands.

i18n bundle organization for new feature: rather than adding a 9th namespace, added the keys under existing namespaces by ergonomic theme (modal copy under modals:redaction.*, sub-toolbar strings under toolbar:redactionTools.*, main toolbar button under toolbar:redact*, menu items under menu:items.redact* + menu:tooltips.redact*). Net delta: ~55 keys across both locales, no schema bump.

jsx-a11y/no-autofocus forbids autoFocus props. Cancel-button-focus-default in a destructive-confirm modal is implemented via useRef + useEffect cancelButtonRef.focus() — satisfies both the design's focus-on-Cancel requirement AND the lint rule. Pattern logged for future destructive-confirm modals.

lesson: Phase 7.4 B1 Redaction review verdict: GO-with-follow-up. R1 rasterize-redact engine ships correctness-clean: U-1 invariant proven structurally (no /Font + /XObject /Image on redacted page), 17/17 sanitize rows covered by rebuild-from-scratch (PDFDocument.create + copyPages drops source catalog by construction), belt-and-braces stripDocLevelJavaScript on output. PAdES gate mirrors ocr-run-on-document.ts (signed_pdf_requires_confirm short-circuits BEFORE engine call; field list flows cleanly). Ravi's SQL writes only invalidated_by_redaction_at — no non-existent column reference. L-001..L-006 all PASS. Findings: 7.4.B1.1 MED (window.pdfApi.pdf as any cast at thunks-phase7-4.ts — Riley parallel-wave coordination scar, cleanable now that David's preload landed), 7.4.B1.2/.3 LOW (engine comment + warning copy), 7.4.B1.4 LOW (AcroForm wholesale-drop UX, v2 candidate). Non-obvious takeaway: when reviewing a security-critical sanitize matrix, ALWAYS spot-check that the engine builds-from-scratch rather than strip-post-hoc — strip-by-construction is structurally stronger because future catalog-level dicts inherit the safety property for free. Second takeaway: parallel-wave `as any` cast is a normal coordination scar at design time but the post-land cleanup MUST be filed as a finding.
fix: For Phase 7.4 B2+ reviews: keep the rebuild-from-scratch vs strip-post-hoc audit as the FIRST sanitize-matrix question. For any parallel-wave `as any` cast left in the renderer after the typed surface lands, file an explicit MED finding so the cleanup PR doesn't slip.

name: Marcus
summary: Caught regression on Julian's PR-CI run: packaged-smoke.spec.ts (Diego's Phase 7.3 candidate from 1eeccc9) had been failing every main-push CI run for 5+ commits — it required release/smoke-v*/win-unpacked/ but CI's e2e job runs before build job, so binary never exists. Diego documented the gating concern in his own commit but the spec ran by default anyway. Fixed by gating describe block behind test.skip(!discoverDefaultExePath()). Exported discoverDefaultExePath from launch-app.ts for the gate. CI green on all four jobs (Windows + Ubuntu check + Playwright e2e + build-skipped-PR-only) at run 27555574795. Branch merged FF to main.
lesson: When a sub-agent's commit documents a CI-gating concern but the agent does NOT actually implement the gating, that documentation alone does NOT prevent the regression. The next CI run (or 5 of them) silently rolls in red. Future review pattern: when an agent's commit-message body says 'this needs CI gating but I didn't add it' OR similar, the orchestrator should add an immediate follow-up dispatch OR file a task. Diego's Phase 7.3 candidate spec sat broken across 5 main-push CI runs before any agent looked at the e2e job results — invisible because nothing else was watching CI.

name: Nathan
summary: Documented Phase 7.4 B1 R1 rasterize-redact across README (+26 net), docs/user-guide.md (+97, new ## Redaction section between Annotating and Importing images), docs/developer-guide.md (+44, new ### Redaction (Phase 7.4 B1) section between Export and Auto-update). Trust-floor language carries the four-location ratchet: (a) user-guide top-of-section warning callout + dedicated Honesty reminder sub-section, (b) Apply modal point-of-action (Riley Wave 2), (c) post-Apply warning toast list, (d) README front-door + Roadmap-status sub-section. Two new keyboard-shortcut rows (Shift+R / Ctrl+Shift+Y) verified against src/client/shortcuts.ts:222-244. Commit 91a2d7f pushed; CI gates GREEN.
takeaways: The seven-times-proven trust-floor four-location ratchet (H-3, Phase 3, Phase 4, Phase 5, Phase 6, Phase 7, now Phase 7.4 B1) holds for destructive-mutation features the same as for security-sensitive ones. Direction of impression is content-agnostic.

Citing engineering takeaways from sibling-agent JSONL entries inside dev-guide architecture sections (David's sanitize-by-construction pattern cite + the 1078669 commit reference) turns post-mortem lessons into forward guidance for the next engine wave.

User-guide trust-floor language for destructive ops needs SCAR-TISSUE framing (documented incidents) up front. Abstract 'this is destructive' messaging is too easy to skim past; naming the failure mode by reference to known incidents makes the reader feel the cost.

name: Marcus
summary: Phase 7.5 plan drafted at docs/project-plan.md (overwrote stale Phase 1 plan; Phase 1 history preserved in git + build-report.md). 9 waves + optional Wave 10 release ceremony. ~96h total engineering. Bucket A (A1-A7) quick wins rolled into Wave 2 alongside the marking-foundation (R1 registry + R2 four contract tests + R3 conventions update). Bucket B reorganized from audit's 10 items to 21 items (B2-B21) to absorb the comparison HTML's partial+missing list. Bucket C: 9 deferred items (Read Aloud, accessibility-authoring tooling, Preflight, HTML/RTF/XML/EPS export, audio comment, PDF Portfolio, multi-doc, native TWAIN, Distribute Form, JS form actions) + 3 ship-overrides (Document Properties dialog, Spell check, Find/Search) with one-line rationale each.
key_decisions: Used registry-additive then UI-cutover commit pattern for R4 risk mitigation (large tool registry retrofit diff).
Bundled B21 Document Properties dialog with B8 Encryption because Properties' Security tab is the natural carrier.
Bundled B20 Sanitize with B5/B8 sanitize family because rebuild-from-scratch is the proven pattern from the 2026-06-15 B1 Redaction learnings.
Sequenced Wave 7 Compare Files last because it's the highest R2 memory-cost risk and benefits from having all engines settled.
Followed audit's §5.4 advice: do NOT propose L-007 lock now; mature for one phase first.
Used the comparison HTML counts (16 missing, 8 partial) as the authoritative scope rather than the audit's older count — the comparison HTML reflects B1 Redaction shipped.
key_lessons: When a project-plan.md is stale (was Phase 1, project is at Phase 7.5), overwriting in place is correct — the file ownership table says project-plan.md is Marcus's, and the durable record of Phase 1 lives in project-roadmap.md + build-report.md + git history. Preserving git history is sufficient; no need to keep stale plans in the working tree.

The audit's Bucket B is a starting point, NOT the scope. Phase 7.5 needed 21 implementation buckets (B2-B21) to cover the comparison HTML's 16-missing + 8-partial list, not just the audit's original 10. Marcus's job is to reconcile the two sources.
The Hard-Won Playbook §1 (parallel-write JSONL contention) maps cleanly onto Phase 7.5 risk R7: with 6 parallel waves each having 2-3 agents, serialize post-flight log writes through Marcus.
Honesty-clause acceptance criterion (#9 in §4) is now baked in per-wave, not just as an inherited norm. Makes Julian's Wave 8 review job easier and the dishonesty cost higher.
DEFER calls need explicit principal-visible disclosure in user-guide (Nathan §9). Hidden deferrals are how marking debt accumulates (per audit §3 stale-tooltip pattern).
open_questions_for_principal: qpdf binary bundling for B8 acceptable (~5-10 MB per OS)?
v0.7.21 cut at phase close or roll to v0.8.0?
L-007 lock now or wait one phase per audit §5.4?

name: David
summary: Phase 7.5 Wave 3 backend engines + IPC: B7 stamp-engine + stamps:list/create/delete + pdf:applyStamp; B4 page-design engines (watermark / header-footer / background); pdf:applyWatermark / pdf:applyHeaderFooter / pdf:applyBackground; dialog:pickFolder helper. Wave 2 carry-over: pdf:splitDocument now returns top-level warnings[] (engine already produced 'bookmarks_not_preserved'). Extended DbBridge with stampsLibrary (10 seeded built-ins in memory bridge; adaptStampsLibraryRepo wraps Ravi's SQLite repo). 43 new vitest tests; full suite 2317/2317 green; typecheck (main+preload+renderer) + lint clean.
key_decisions: Stamp engine ships content-stream draw, NOT /Stamp annotations. Brief preferred /Stamp annotations but pdf-lib's public API doesn't expose a clean annotation builder + rasterize-on-demand requires pdf.js which Wave 3 deliberately avoids. Same trade-off watermark/H&F/redact already made; engine docs note this honestly. Annotation-based stamps deferred to Wave 6+.

Background engine emits a permanent 'background_rendered_over_content' warning. pdf-lib's drawRectangle/drawImage append to content-stream tail so backgrounds layer ON TOP of existing content (not behind). Honest disclosure surfaces in the response.warnings[] so Riley's UI can render the limitation.

Directory tokens added as a sibling namespace to SaveDestination on documentStore (60s TTL, distinct map). Reuses the existing SaveDestination pattern but with its own consume path so a folder token cannot be mis-consumed as a save-as token.

Stamps wired into DbBridge as a non-nullable slot (vs Phase 5/6 pattern of nullable slot until repo ships). The memory bridge seeds 10 built-ins to mirror migration 0009 seed rows so production and test paths look identical.
key_lessons: When the brief asks for two trade-off options ('/Stamp annotation' vs 'content-stream draw'), the honest choice is the one that matches existing patterns and ships in-scope. Picking 'rasterize-on-demand annotations' would have required pdf.js plumbing the wave explicitly tells us NOT to add. Engine file headers must document the deferred choice + the wave it belongs to.

Riley's untracked WIP (stamps-slice.ts, stamps-api.ts, region-clipboard-slice.ts) is on disk but not committed; baseline-comparison via 'git stash' misses these because stash skips untracked unless -u is passed. Running the baseline test with `git stash -u` shows 204/204 / 2274 tests; after my work + Riley's WIP it's 211/211 / 2317 (+43 mine).

Adding a new field to a non-frozen PdfApi namespace requires updating the renderer-side fallback in src/client/services/api.ts. The boundary is documented as 'mirror the contract' so this is the contract owner's legitimate touch even under strict file ownership. Failure mode: TS2741 on renderer typecheck.

pdf-lib's embedPng rejects byte sequences that look like PNGs but have a malformed IHDR (e.g. RGBA color-type 6 + a hand-constructed IDAT with wrong filter byte). The minimal valid PNG that passes is a real 1x1 RGB pixel with a proper deflate stream — easier to generate via zlib at script time than to hand-write the bytes.

name: David
summary: Phase 7.5 Wave 4 backend: B6 Compress (compress-engine.ts, rebuild-from-scratch per P7.5-L-12; honest warnings for non-implemented image/font knobs; no new deps — sharp NOT vendored), B13 Hyperlinks (link-engine.ts, low-level PDFDict /Annot /Subtype /Link authoring; URI / goto-page /Fit|/FitH|/XYZ / goto-bookmark via private /ConductorBookmarkId; stable per-page-index linkIds), B19 Auto-bookmarks (auto-bookmark-engine.ts, pure pdf.js-free font-size cluster heuristic with deps-injected extractor; production extractor wired in register.ts via the canonical loadPdfJs+tryLoadCanvas pattern; L-004/L-005 compliant). Three handlers + three engines + six test files (48 tests). Contracts.ts adds PdfCompressDocument/PdfAutoBookmarkFromHeadings/PdfEditLinks types, channel ids, and PdfApi.pdf methods. Preload exposes the three new methods. Commit d837091. ~4h vs estimate ~5h.
key_decisions: Compress engine ships rebuild-from-scratch ONLY (no sharp, no image walking). Brief said 'use sharp IF needed; otherwise pdf-lib's built-in mechanisms first' — pdf-lib's useObjectStreams: true + rebuild via copyPages discards orphan objects AND packs the survivors, which delivers the brief's '1064-page PDF in reasonable time' gate honestly. Adding sharp would have pulled ~10MB of native binaries for what the principal hasn't (yet) asked for. Warnings surface for image-downsample / JPEG-quality / font-subset so the renderer's UI can show 'requested but deferred' honestly.

Link engine uses pdf-lib LOW-LEVEL dict API (PDFDict.withContext + PDFName.of + page.node.lookupMaybe('Annots', PDFArray)) rather than pdf-lib's high-level form/annotation builders. Reason: pdf-lib does NOT expose a /Link annotation builder, and the manual path mirrors the field-dict-authoring.ts pattern that already handles signature placeholders. Stable linkIds of the form '<pageIndex>:<annotIndex>' survive list→edit→list round-trips provided no other agent touches the doc between calls.

Auto-bookmark engine takes a DEPS-INJECTED text extractor instead of importing pdf.js directly. The handler bridges the gap — in register.ts I wired a productionExtractPageTextItems closure that lazily loads pdf.js via the same ocr-bootstrap loadPdfJs path (tryLoadCanvas() installs canvas globals BEFORE the dynamic import resolves per L-005; new Uint8Array(bytes).slice() passes an OWNED copy to getDocument per L-004). This way the ENGINE module stays pdf.js-free + unit-testable without a canvas binding.

Validation of maxDepth: engine accepts maxDepth >= 1 and CLAMPS internally to [1,6] via clampInt. The IPC handler's zod schema enforces the strict [1,6] upper bound at the caller-facing surface. Splits 'caller-protection' (handler schema) from 'engine-correctness' (clamp) and lets the engine stay forgiving for direct unit callers.
key_lessons: When parallel-wave coordination with Riley (the renderer-owning teammate) produces renderer typecheck failures, the strict file-ownership rule wins: I do NOT edit src/client/. Coordination delta (api.ts unavailable-fallback stub needs 3 new methods) flows back to Marcus / Riley via the commit message + post-flight report. Wave 3 (commit b2b475b) had the same pattern; Riley pre-wired fallback stubs that same wave. This time Riley's brief presumably didn't cover the Wave 4 contract additions, so the gap surfaces as one transient renderer-typecheck failure until Riley follows up.

'git stash --include-untracked' BEHAVES unexpectedly when popping — the tracked-file modifications can silently fail to restore while the new untracked files come back. After 'stash pop' I had to MANUALLY re-apply contracts.ts + register.ts + preload/index.ts edits (the new engine + handler + test files survived because they were untracked-and-restored-as-untracked, but the contract diffs were silently dropped). Lesson: prefer 'git stash push -- <paths>' + 'git stash pop' for narrow comparisons; for whole-tree snapshots, copy the files first or use a worktree. Don't stash mid-wave at all if you can avoid it.

exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true + zod inferred types: zod's z.union with optional fields produces 'zoom?: ... | undefined' in the inferred type, which is NOT assignable to a contract type that defines 'zoom?: ...' (no undefined). Fix: declare a local ZodLinkAction / ZodLinkTarget interface in the handler that mirrors the zod-inferred shape (with '| undefined' on optionals), then translate via conditional spread when mapping to the engine type. Avoids 'as any' AND keeps the engine surface strict.

writeTarget helper pattern: returning 'null | Result<true, E>' confuses TypeScript when the caller is 'Result<string, E>'. Cleaner: return 'null | { error: E; message: string }' (a bare error envelope) and let the call site wrap with fail<E>(...). One-character change at the call site, much clearer type story.

name: Riley
summary: Phase 7.5 Wave 4 renderer: B4 Page Design three-tab modal (Watermark + Header & Footer + Background) with preview cartoon, range picker, dispatching David's Wave 3 channels; B13 Hyperlinks (LinksOverlay + AddLinkModal + linksSlice + linksApi, dispatches David's Wave 4 pdf:editLinks; tooltip-on-hover badges, right-click Edit/Remove context menu, marquee draw tool armed by Shift+L); ShapeDrawOverlay closes Wave 3 open-question #3 (line / polyline / area-measure now draw on pdf-canvas via the existing slice + buildShapeAnnotationFromDraft pipeline); region-clipboard CUT scope-reduced to disabled-with-tooltip (B20 sanitize follow-up). Tool registry adds four entries (pages:watermark, pages:header-footer, pages:background, annotation:add-link) keeping the L-007 ratchet candidate clean. Typecheck + lint + 2365 vitest tests green (including the four marking-contract tests over the new tool entries). ~3h vs estimate ~3h.
key_decisions: Mounted the ShapeDrawOverlay at the app level (sibling of RegionClipboardOverlay) rather than inside PdfCanvas. PdfCanvas is per-page and the shape pointer lifecycle spans pages during a drag (a polygon can run off the source page's bbox). App-level mount catches pointer-move beyond the originating page and the slice carries pageIndex through to commit. Mirrors the RegionClipboard pattern; symmetrically the existing ShapeDraftOverlay remains the visual SVG.

Adopted David's canonical LinkTarget shape (uri / goto-page / goto-bookmark with bookmarkId) DIRECTLY in the renderer slice rather than translating renderer-local 'url' to wire 'uri' at the IPC boundary. One fewer translation point + the renderer reads David's contract first-hand. The internal stub file (links-contract-stub.ts) became a thin re-export through the gatekeeper after David's Wave 4 contracts landed in lockstep with this wave.

Preview-pane is an HONEST CARTOON (CSS-rendered approximation of the page-design overlay), NOT a pdf.js rasterized preview. Real per-keystroke re-raster would violate the 1064-page perf gate (acceptance #4). Cartoon validates position / opacity / rotation / color; engine produces the truth on Apply. Honest scope is in the file header.

Used Shift+L for the Add Link shortcut rather than plain 'L'. Plain L is already the existing Line / Arrow shape tool on the shape sub-toolbar; reusing it would clobber the line tool when a doc is open. Pattern matches polyline-measure (Shift+M) and area-measure (Shift+A).

CUT scope-reduce: rendered disabled-with-tooltip + enabledWhen:()=>false in the registry. Same Phase-7.4-A1 'dishonest placeholder removal' pattern Marcus codified. The tooltip explicitly names the upcoming B20 sanitize wave so users see the reason.
key_lessons: Parallel-wave coordination scar: my brief said 'if David's contracts are missing pieces, leave typed stub + open question'. Reality: David's Wave 4 contracts (pdf:editLinks, pdf:compressDocument, pdf:autoBookmarkFromHeadings) landed THE SAME WAVE as mine via a parallel commit (d837091). Net: the renderer's api.ts unavailable-fallback was missing the three new methods, breaking renderer typecheck. Fix: add the three fallback entries in api.ts and re-export the new contract types through src/client/types/ipc-contract.ts. David's wave-4 entry calls out the same coordination delta from his side. Lesson: when contracts.ts changes upstream, the renderer's api.ts fallback + ipc-contract gatekeeper re-export are mandatory companion edits even under strict file ownership — they're 'mirror the contract' touches that exist because of the renderer's typecheck boundary, not because of file-ownership creep.

i18n bundle editing under a lint:fix run: eslint --fix MUTATES our locale JSON files in ways that can drop keys silently when a sibling tool revisits them. After lint:fix I had to RE-APPLY watermark / addLink / pageDesign keys to en-US and es-ES toolbar.json and modals.json THREE times. Lesson: when adding a large i18n bundle, run lint:fix EARLY (against a small sample) to flush its formatter through, then add all keys in one big edit and re-run lint (NOT lint:fix) to confirm. Otherwise the formatter can wipe additions when triggered against an in-progress diff. Documented this pattern for the swarm via this lesson.

exactOptionalPropertyTypes + zod + Wave-3-shipped contract: PdfApplyHeaderFooterRequest has 'header?: PdfHeaderFooterStrip' (NOT undefined-permitted). 'header: hasContent ? value : undefined' fails the check; '...(hasContent ? { header: value } : {})' conditional-spread is the canonical pattern. Same lesson David flagged in this wave on his side for zod-vs-contract differences.
open_questions: Live pdf.js rasterized preview in the Page Design modal: deferred for v0.8.0 (perf gate); plausible v0.9.x.

Hyperlink linkId reconciliation: I generate renderer-side IDs via uuid; David's engine returns its own linkIds. The session-local renderer tracks the renderer-id; future patch should reconcile after first Apply to use the engine's id (right now updates/removes work against the renderer id even when a save-round-trip has invalidated it).

Operator-level visual verification of the Page Design modal applied to a real 5-page test PDF is NOT done in this dispatch (no interactive Electron session available). Type-strict + 2365 tests + registry contract gate covers correctness; the live screenshot will come in Wave 11 packaging.

task: Phase 7.5 Wave 5 — B8 encryption + B20 sanitize + B18 font-swap + B21 doc-properties + Wave-4 carry-over link /Dest fix
detail: Five engines + IPC contracts/handlers/preload + tests; all typecheck + lint + 2401-test vitest suite green. Three non-obvious takeaways: (1) pdf-lib's setKeywords(arr) joins with SPACE, not comma — so a round-trip via setKeywords([a,b,c]) → getKeywords() yields the original SINGLE-STRING 'a b c'. The parseKeywords reader needed an explicit whitespace-fallback when no comma/semicolon is present. (2) PDFDocument.load(bytes) defaults updateMetadata: true which RE-WRITES /Info Producer to 'pdf-lib (https://github.com/Hopding/pdf-lib)' on load. Any test that asserts the engine's setProducer survives MUST load with {updateMetadata: false}. Same pattern is encoded in redact-engine.test.ts but not visible from the type signature. (3) The eslint no-control-regex rule blocks ANY literal control char in a regex, even when you intentionally WANT to strip control chars from untrusted subprocess stderr. Build the char class via new RegExp(`[${String.fromCharCode(0)...String.fromCharCode(0x1f).join('')}]`) — same runtime behavior, no eslint disable.
fix: src/main/pdf-ops/document-properties.ts parseKeywords; src/main/pdf-ops/sanitize-engine.test.ts load{updateMetadata:false}; src/main/pdf-ops/encryption-engine.ts sanitizeStderr

name: Riley
summary: Phase 7.5 Wave 5 renderer: B21 Document Properties dialog (Description tab editable + Security tab B8 password protection + Fonts/Custom honest placeholders); B20 Sanitize alertdialog (9 v0.8.0 supported categories, default-checked baseline, select-all/none bulk row, PAdES gate handling); B19 Auto-bookmark UI two-step (confirm heuristic + maxDepth → review editable tree + replace/append merge mode, dispatches David's Wave 4 pdf:autoBookmarkFromHeadings then bookmarks:upsert per row). Three new modals mounted in app.tsx, three new slices wired in store.ts, three registry tool entries (file:properties, tools:sanitize, bookmarks:auto-bookmark) pass the four marking-contract tests. New shortcut 'file-properties' (Ctrl+D, Acrobat parity) dispatched through use-app-shortcuts.ts. Menu mirrors land on File → Properties... and Tools → Remove Hidden Information.... Bookmarks panel grows an 'Auto-generate Bookmarks...' button. Typecheck (renderer + main + preload + root) + lint --max-warnings 0 + 2401 vitest tests green (up from 2365 Wave 4). ~3h vs estimate ~4h.
key_decisions: Renderer-local contract stub files (document-properties-contract-stub.ts + sanitize-contract-stub.ts) declared inline rather than blocking on David's parallel contracts.ts commit. Thunks call David's channels via feature-detect on window.pdfApi?.pdf?.<name>, identical pattern to Phase 7.4 B1 redactions thunk (thunks-phase7-4.ts). Result: my renderer compiled and ran independently of David's commit timing; AFTER his commit landed the official types coexist with my stubs and both work. The stub files have explicit promotion notes for a future re-export-wrapper edit (Wave 6 cleanup) but typecheck is honest TODAY.

Document Properties tab structure shipped four tabs (Description + Security + Fonts + Custom) per ui-spec §21 — but Fonts and Custom render an HONEST 'Coming in a later release' placeholder instead of being missing. Skipped tabs disappear; honest placeholder + i18n key documents the deferral. Matches the trust-floor pattern (eight-times-proven).

Security tab is BUNDLED into Document Properties per architecture P7.5-L-2 ('B8 carries Document Properties dialog Security tab'), not a stand-alone modal. The Apply Security button + Apply (Description) button live on different tabs so they don't visually compete. Matches Acrobat's behavior + the ui-spec §8.1 wireframe.

Sanitize modal uses role='alertdialog' + cancelButtonRef-default-focus per the redaction Apply-modal pattern (Phase 7.4 B1). 9 v0.8.0 supported categories surface as checkboxes; the other 8 (object-data, thumbnails, etc.) sit at false for forward compatibility. Default-checked baseline matches the most-common 'scrub before sharing' workflow (metadata + js + deleted-content + hidden-text + hidden-layers default ON; attachments / comments / form-fields / bookmarks / links default OFF). PAdES gate response handled inline (no separate modal): when David's engine returns 'signed_pdf_requires_confirm', the field-name list renders in a red-bordered alert + the user re-clicks Sanitize to confirm. Same shape as the redaction thunk.

Auto-bookmark slice carries an editable AutoBookmarkRow[] (id + title + pageIndex + depth + deleted flag) rather than mutating the engine's ProposedBookmark[] directly. The deleted flag is reversible (undo via row's ↺ button) and is filtered at save time. Replace vs append merge mode mirrors Acrobat. Save calls bookmarks.upsert per row with a depth->parent stack so the saved hierarchy reflects the heuristic's nesting.
key_lessons: Same parallel-wave-coordination scar Riley flagged in Wave 4 surfaced HERE TOO: David's Wave 5 commit landed contracts.ts + preload + api.ts fallback during my work. Because my thunks feature-detect on window.pdfApi.pdf.<channel> (NOT the api.ts typed proxy), my code compiles BOTH BEFORE and AFTER David's commit lands. The stubs in src/client/types/{document-properties,sanitize}-contract-stub.ts shape the renderer's slice / form state and are not blocked by David's contracts arriving. Promotion to canonical re-exports is a Wave 6 cleanup edit (mirrors the Wave 4 links-contract-stub.ts promotion). Lesson: when David has not yet landed canonical types, soft-feature-detect dispatch + renderer-local stub types is the cleanest parallel-coordination pattern. The api.ts proxy is reserved for channels whose canonical types DEFINITELY exist at compile time.

Slice 'open' flag is THE right pattern for modals that compose with each other. Document Properties + Sanitize + Auto-bookmark each have a dedicated 'open' boolean on their dedicated slice (not ui.activeModal). This lets multiple modal pipelines coexist (e.g. PAdES gate alertdialog inside Sanitize while Document Properties stays mounted in background). Single ui.activeModal forces mutual exclusion which the brief explicitly does NOT want for these compound flows.

useT() returns { t, locale } — NOT { t, i18n } (no i18next instance is exposed). New Date(...).toLocaleString(locale) works correctly with the AppLocale string. The first typecheck error of this wave was a wrong import shape on the description tab; the fix is one-line. Worth documenting because the pattern is common and the error message ('Property i18n does not exist on type ...') is misleading.
open_questions: David's Wave 5 PdfRemoveHiddenInfoError union does NOT include 'signed_pdf_requires_confirm' (the redaction precedent does). My UI surfaces a PAdES gate panel anyway because the slice state's pendingInvalidatedSignatureFields field is structurally identical to the redaction slice. At runtime that branch is dead code today — fine, but the next iteration should either (a) align David's engine to return the gate signal, or (b) drop the PAdES panel from the Sanitize modal. Logged for the cross-roster sync.

David's PdfSetDocumentPropertiesValue includes 'warnings: string[]' that my thunk does NOT currently surface as toasts. Honest warnings should fan to pushToast in a follow-up. Same for setPasswordProtection's value (also adds warnings).

Operator-level visual verification of the three new modals against a real PDF: NOT done this dispatch (no interactive Electron session available; the renderer would hit bridge_unavailable for all three channels until David's preload bridge entries deploy at runtime). Type-strict + 2401 tests + registry contract gate covers correctness; the live screenshot lands in Wave 11 packaging. Acceptable per the 'visual verification is required when surface ships' precedent (parallel-wave deferral is the established norm).

name: David
summary: Phase 7.5 Wave 5a backend: C1 Read Aloud (TTS) engine + per-OS adapters (SAPI/PowerShell on Windows, say on macOS, espeak SUBPROCESS-ONLY on Linux), tts:listVoices/speakText/pause/resume/stop IPC + tts:boundary push channel. C2 Preflight rules engine (pure pdf-lib, no new deps) with shared helpers + 10 representative rules across PDF/X-1a, PDF/X-4, PDF/A-1b, PDF/A-2b profiles + pdf:runPreflight IPC. Wave 5 carry-over: signed_pdf_requires_confirm variant in sanitize-engine + handler + contracts (Riley's UI panel already renders it). Contracts.ts adds TTS/Preflight types + Channels enum entries. Preload exposes new methods + onBoundary listener. 26 new tests (TtsEngine + 3 adapters + preflight engine). typecheck (main + preload + renderer) green; lint clean; full 2429-test vitest suite green. ~3h vs estimate ~6h (came in well under because per-OS adapters share the same subprocess shape and preflight rules are tiny ≤40-line files).
key_decisions: TTS adapters wire a SpawnFn injection seam so unit tests verify exact argv vectors without spawning real PowerShell / say / espeak. Each adapter exports a createXAdapter({ spawnFn? }) factory; the engine constructor accepts the same. Default = child_process.spawn. This is the canonical 'subprocess-mocking pattern' the brief asked for and matches the cross-project conductor playbook (Hard-Won Playbook #19 for the operator side).

Windows pause/resume is a NO-OP (returns state:paused/resumed without affecting the PowerShell child). POSIX SIGSTOP/SIGCONT pauses the actual process tree; Windows has no equivalent and stop+restart-from-offset is deferred per the spec. The UI still surfaces the affordance — honest 'best-effort highlight' UX without a misleading 'broken pause button'.

Preflight ships 10 rules (5 cross-profile × 4 profiles + 5 per-profile = 25 instantiations of 10 unique rule modules). Spec §3 enumerates ~30; the framework is in place + the highest-leverage rules are wired. ALL_PREFLIGHT_RULES.length feeds the shippedRuleCount on the response so any future addition flows automatically — and the engine test asserts the count tracks the registry. Honest-disclosure regression catches drift between Nathan's docs and the code at the test boundary.

One file per rule (≤40 lines each, well under the ≤200 budget) + a thin index.ts that re-exports. Cross-profile rules use a factory pattern (PROFILES.map(p => ({...}))) so adding a profile to a rule is a one-element array edit. Per-profile rules export a single PreflightRule constant. Both shapes coexist cleanly in the ALL_PREFLIGHT_RULES array.
key_lessons: exactOptionalPropertyTypes + zod-inferred optional fields: handler unwraps z.optional() fields and the zod-inferred type carries '| undefined'; passing those into a strict optional-property type fails. Pattern (now used in Waves 4, 5, 5a): unwrap with conditional spread — `const opts = { text, sentenceBoundaries, ...(d.voiceId !== undefined ? { voiceId: d.voiceId } : {}), ...(d.rate !== undefined ? { rate: d.rate } : {}) }`. Same shape for the engine's adapter spawnFn arg, and for the sanitize signed-doc opt-in. Three sites in this wave alone — this idiom is now muscle memory.

pdf-lib's `lookupMaybe` overloads support PDFDict / PDFStream / PDFArray / PDFRef / PDFName / PDFString / PDFHexString / PDFBool / PDFNumber / PDFNull and ONE two-type union (PDFString | PDFHexString) and ONE three-type union (above + PDFArray). PDFRawStream is NOT a valid overload (must use PDFStream — which is the parent). XMP metadata streams resolve via `catalog.lookupMaybe(PDFName.of('Metadata'), PDFStream).getContents()`. Burned ~10 min on the wrong overload; documenting the supported set here for the next backend-engineer dispatch.

ESLint flags U+FEFF (zero-width no-break space, the XMP BOM marker) under 'no-irregular-whitespace'. Test fixtures that need XMP content should use a literal empty between the begin='' quotes or build the BOM via String.fromCharCode(0xFEFF) at runtime — the literal in source code burns CI even when the rendered fixture is byte-correct.

ESLint's import/order auto-fix sorts alphabetically AND collapses comment-delimited subgroups. If you want logical grouping (cross-profile rules together, per-profile rules together) you have two options: (a) rely on the autosort and group ONLY in the consumer array (what I did — ALL_PREFLIGHT_RULES groups by intent), (b) write all imports in a single block with a single grouping comment instead of multiple subgroup comments. Option (a) keeps fix-on-save clean.

summary: Read Aloud floating bar (View/Ctrl+Alt+R) with Play/Pause/Resume/Stop, voice picker, rate slider, sentence progress, engine-unavailable honest fallback. Preflight sidebar panel as 7th tab with profile checkboxes, Run button (NO eager-run), severity-bucketed result sections, page-navigation on row click, JSON export. Subset disclosure permanent in panel header. Brief said Shift+Ctrl+R but that chord collides with the existing rotate-ccw — shipped Ctrl+Alt+R + logged the open question for Marcus. takeaways: When the brief's specified chord collides with an existing Phase-1 binding, ship a non-conflicting variant and log the chord-migration question rather than silently shadow the existing dispatcher.

David's canonical contracts.ts is the source of truth for IPC field names — the api-contracts.md draft used speakId/onProgress but contracts.ts uses jobId/onBoundary. Always cross-check contracts.ts before committing the stub.

The fallback factory in services/api.ts must include every namespace declared on the canonical PdfApi shape — David already added `tts` + `pdf.runPreflight` to the canonical type, so the renderer fallback would have failed to satisfy the type if I'd skipped them. Even with feature-detected runtime dispatch, the typed `PdfApi` return narrows the renderer surface.
